paragraph_text: Hyundai Elevator Push Button

Hyundai elevator push button is the main contact point used by passengers to select floors and request lift movement. When pressed, the button sends a signal to the elevator control system which then manages travel direction and stopping points. This process supports safe and organised passenger movement inside buildings.

In elevators manufactured by Hyundai, push buttons are designed to match system voltage, panel layout and control logic. Each button is made for clear response, stable use and correct fitting within the elevator panel. Reliable push buttons are essential for smooth lift service in the UAE where elevators run daily in residential and commercial buildings.

The Hyundai elevator push button supports both car calls and hall calls. Car call buttons are used inside the lift cabin to select floors. Hall call buttons are used on each landing to request upward or downward travel. Both types must work without delay to avoid confusion and service issues.
Available Hyundai Push Button Types
Our Hyundai elevator push button range includes various button models designed for elevator control panels and operating systems, including:

• Push Button 24V - A4J16002 A2
  • Push Button AK32 - Installation Hole Size 34
  • Push Button AK-4B - Installation Hole Size 33
These Hyundai elevator push buttons are available in different configurations to support various car call and hall call applications within elevator passenger interface systems.
Product Applications
Safe applications based on the client input:

• Car call applications
  • Hall call applications
  • Car operating panels (COP)
  • Landing operating panels (LOP)
  • Elevator passenger interface systems
  • Elevator maintenance and replacement requirements
FAQ

1. How does a passenger communicate a floor request in an elevator?
Passengers use elevator push buttons to register travel requests within the elevator system.

2. Are push buttons used in both COP and LOP panels?
Yes. Push buttons are commonly installed in both Car Operating Panels (COP) and Landing Operating Panels (LOP).

3. What is the purpose of a hall call button?
A hall call button allows passengers to request elevator service from a floor landing.

4. Can individual push buttons be replaced without changing the entire panel?
Push buttons are commonly supplied as individual replacement components for maintenance requirements.

5. Why do push buttons have different mounting specifications?
Mounting specifications vary to match different elevator panel designs and installation requirements.

paragraph_text: Hyundai Elevator Door Roller

The Hyundai elevator door roller is an essential part of a Hyundai lift door system. It supports safe and smooth door movement during every opening and closing cycle. These rollers are designed to guide the door panel along its track with steady motion and low noise.
Lift doors operate in a controlled and reliable way that meets daily building use needs when the correct roller is used and fitted properly.

Door rollers are also known as door wheels. They carry the door panel weight and reduces direct contact between the door and the guide rail. This helps limit wear on the door system and supports long service life. In Hyundai elevators, using the right roller model is important for correct alignment and safe door travel.

  • Car door assemblies
  • Landing door assemblies

Yes. The terms door roller and door wheel are commonly used to describe the same elevator door component.

2. What are the common applications of elevator door rollers?
Door rollers are used within elevator door systems to support the movement of door panels during opening and closing operations.

A door roller supports the movement of the door panel during opening and closing operations within the elevator door assembly.

paragraph_text: Hyundai Elevator Guide Shoe Insert

The Hyundai elevator guide shoe insert is a small, removable component that makes direct contact with the elevator guide rail. Installed within the guide shoe assembly, it forms part of the elevator guiding system during normal lift operation.

As the contact material between the guide shoe assembly and the guide rail, the guide shoe insert is an important component used across Hyundai elevator systems. Different insert sizes are available to match specific guide shoe configurations and maintenance requirements.
It can be replaced during servicing without changing the complete guide shoe assembly because the insert is removable. This helps simplify maintenance and replacement activities when required.
Available Hyundai Guide Shoe Insert Types
Our Hyundai elevator guide shoe insert range includes:

• Guide Shoe Insert - 120 × 22 × 6
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 80 × 50 × 57 × 7

These guide shoe inserts are used as the contact material between the guide shoe assembly and the elevator guide rail. Different insert sizes are available to match specific elevator guide shoe configurations.
Product Applications
Hyundai elevator guide shoe insert components are commonly associated with:

• Guide shoe assemblies
  • Elevator guide rail contact applications
  • Elevator guiding systems
  • Elevator maintenance activities
  • Replacement requirements
  • Residential, commercial and industrial elevator applications
FAQ

1. Why are guide shoe inserts designed as removable components?
Removable guide shoe inserts allow the contact material to be replaced without changing the complete guide shoe assembly.

2. What is the difference between a guide shoe and a guide shoe insert?
The guide shoe is the complete assembly, while the guide shoe insert is the replaceable component that makes contact with the guide rail.

3. Why are guide shoe inserts available in different dimensions?
Different guide shoe assemblies require specific insert sizes to match their design and installation requirements.

4. Can guide shoe inserts be replaced without changing the full guide shoe?
Yes. Guide shoe inserts are removable components that can typically be replaced independently.

5. When should a guide shoe insert be inspected?
Guide shoe inserts are commonly checked during routine elevator maintenance and servicing activities.

paragraph_text: Hyundai Elevator Switches and Sensors

Within an elevator system, switches and sensors provide information related to position, movement and load conditions. Hyundai elevator switches and sensors include components such as limit switches, load sensors and proximity switches, each supporting a specific monitoring function during lift operation.

From defining travel limits to detecting metal targets and monitoring elevator load, these components support various monitoring and safety-related functions across Hyundai elevator systems. They are commonly used during elevator maintenance, inspection and replacement activities in residential and commercial buildings.

At Bestech UAE, we specialize in supplying genuine Hyundai elevator spare parts. Based in Sharjah, we provide reliable switches and sensors for residential, commercial and industrial sectors with expert support.

FAQ

1. What does a Hyundai elevator limit switch do?
A limit switch acts as a safety sensor to make sure the elevator car does not travel past its safe boundaries.

2. Why are load sensors important in a lift system?
Load sensors measure the weight inside the elevator car and support load monitoring applications.

3. How do proximity switches work in Hyundai elevators?
They are electronic sensors that detect metal targets such as steel plates and guide rails without physical contact.

Yes. The switches and sensors are designed for standard Hyundai elevator setups in various building types.

Yes. These are commonly sourced by technicians for standard repairs, maintenance and safety inspections.

Installed inside elevator control panels, Hyundai PCB boards support electronic communication and controller functions within lift systems. These elevator PCB boards are suitable for maintenance, replacement and elevator control applications in residential and commercial buildings.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Push Button

A KONE elevator push button is a key component used for passenger interaction within an elevator system. Installed in both elevator cars and landing panels, these buttons allow users to register floor selections and elevator calls while supporting smooth passenger movement throughout the building.

As part of the elevator signalization system, KONE push buttons are designed to combine functionality with user-friendly operation. Different button designs are available to suit various elevator models, panel configurations and building requirements. Their design also supports accessibility and visual integration, helping maintain a consistent appearance within modern elevator interiors.

Used across residential, commercial, hospitality and public buildings, KONE elevator push buttons play an important role in daily elevator operation by providing a clear and reliable interface between passengers and the elevator control system.
Available KONE Push Button Options
These push buttons are suitable for various KONE elevator systems and panel configurations, including:

• KD550 round push buttons
  • KDS300 round push buttons
  • KDS300 square push buttons
  • Elevator car operating panel (COP) push buttons
  • Elevator landing operating panel (LOP) push buttons
  • Floor selection push buttons
  • Elevator call buttons
  • Elevator button plates
Functions
KONE elevator push buttons support essential passenger communication and elevator operation functions, including:

• Registering floor selection requests
  • Calling the elevator from landing floors
  • Providing passenger input to the elevator control system
  • Supporting elevator navigation and passenger interaction within the building
  • Enhancing user interaction through organised panel layouts
  • Provide smooth and efficient elevator operation
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a KONE elevator push button?
A KONE elevator push button allows passengers to interact with the elevator system by selecting floors and registering elevator calls.

2. What is the difference between COP and LOP push buttons?
COP push buttons are installed inside the elevator car for floor selection, while LOP push buttons are installed on each landing floor to call the elevator.

3. Are KONE elevator push buttons available in different shapes and designs?
Yes. KONE elevator push buttons are available in different designs, including round and square button configurations, depending on the elevator system and panel requirements.

4. How do elevator push buttons improve passenger usability?
Elevator push buttons provide a clear method for passengers to register floor selections and elevator calls, supporting convenient system interaction.

5. What factors should be considered when selecting a replacement KONE push button?
Important factors include the elevator model, panel type, button dimensions, mounting configuration and compatibility with the existing system.

groups.meta_description: Find KONE elevator door roller sheave components, including door rollers, car door rollers, sheaves and related parts for elevator door and traction systems.
groups.keywords: KONE Elevator Door Rollers and Sheaves, KONE Door Roller Parts, Elevator Door Roller Sheaves UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Door Roller & Sheave

KONE elevator door rollers and sheaves are important mechanical components used in elevator door systems and traction mechanisms. These parts support the smooth movement of elevator doors and help maintain reliable elevator operation within residential, commercial and public buildings.

Door rollers are used to guide and support the movement of elevator car doors and landing doors during opening and closing cycles. Their role helps reduce friction, support door alignment and contribute to consistent door operation.

Sheaves are grooved wheel components used within traction elevator systems. They work together with hoist ropes or belts to support the movement of the elevator car and counterweight. As part of the elevator drive mechanism, sheaves play an important role in maintaining controlled elevator travel and system performance.
Available KONE Door Roller and Sheave Options
The available range includes door rollers, car door rollers, and sheave components in various specifications:
Door Rollers

• Ø68 × 17 × M10
  • Ø94 × 19 × M8
  • Ø94 × 19 × M10
  • Ø85 × 17 × 6203
  • Ø85 × Ø76 × 12 × 6203
  • Ø85 × 17 × Ø10
Car Door Rollers

• Ø60 × 14 × M8
  • Ø64 × Ø39 × 16 × M12
Sheaves and Sheave Components

• Ø45 × 11 × Ø10
  • Ø48 × 8 × Ø10
  • Ø116 × 12 × Ø10
  • Ø45 × 11 × Ø10 (Sheave Component)
Note: Review the roller dimensions and bearing configuration to identify a suitable replacement.
FAQ

1. What is the function of a KONE elevator door roller?
A KONE elevator door roller helps guide and support the movement of elevator doors during opening and closing operations.

2. What does a sheave do in a KONE elevator system?
A sheave is a grooved wheel component that works with hoist ropes or belts to support elevator car movement within a traction elevator system.

3. Are door rollers and sheaves used in different parts of the elevator system?
Yes. Door rollers are typically used within elevator door mechanisms, while sheaves are used within the traction and drive system.

4. What can happen when elevator door rollers become worn?
Worn door rollers may affect door movement, alignment, or overall door operation, making timely replacement important for system performance.

5. Why is sheave condition important in traction elevators?
The sheave works directly with hoist ropes or belts, making it an important component in elevator movement and traction system operation.

faq.answer: A sheave is a grooved wheel component that works with hoist ropes or belts to support elevator car movement within a traction elevator system.

faq.answer: Worn door rollers may affect door movement, alignment, or overall door operation, making timely replacement important for system performance.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Guide Shoe Roller

Elevator cars and counterweights travel along fixed guide rails throughout the hoistway. The KONE elevator guide shoe roller is one of the components involved in this guided movement, helping the elevator system follow its intended travel path. Installed within guide shoe assemblies, these rollers are used in various KONE elevator applications and are selected according to elevator model and system requirements.
As elevator systems may use different guide assemblies, guide shoe rollers are supplied in a range of specifications to accommodate various installation requirements.
Available KONE Guide Shoe Roller Options
The available guide shoe roller range includes the following specifications:

• Ø80 × 25 mm (6203)
  • Ø80 × 28 mm (2 × 6203)
  • Ø125 × 24 mm (M12 Shaft)
  • Ø150 × 38 mm (2 × 6003)
  • Ø150 × 28 mm (2 × 6203)
  • Ø200 × 30 mm (6007)
Note: Please verify the outer diameter (Ø), roller width and bearing specifications before selecting a replacement guide shoe roller.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator system, guide shoe rollers play a role in:

• Elevator car guidance
  • Counterweight guidance
  • Alignment along guide rails
  • Vertical travel support
  • Guide shoe assembly operation
  • Elevator guidance system movement
FAQ

1. What is the difference between a guide shoe and a guide shoe roller?
A guide shoe is the assembly that interfaces with the guide rail, while the guide shoe roller is one of the components used within certain roller guide arrangements.

2. What measurements should be checked before ordering a replacement guide shoe roller?
Important measurements typically include the outer diameter, roller width, shaft size and bearing specification.

3. Can guide shoe rollers be selected using dimensions alone?
Dimensions are useful for identification, but part numbers and elevator model information should also be verified whenever possible.

4. Are guide shoe rollers used for both elevator cars and counterweights?
Depending on the elevator design, guide shoe rollers may be used in guide assemblies associated with the elevator car, counterweight or both.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator roller guide shoe assembly

KONE elevator roller guide shoe assemblies are available in a range of roller sizes and bearing configurations to suit different elevator applications. These assemblies are selected based on the specifications of the existing component and are commonly used during maintenance and replacement work.
To identify a suitable replacement, it is recommended to verify the roller dimensions, bearing details, and existing part specifications before ordering.
Available KONE Roller Guide Shoe Assembly Options
The available KONE elevator roller guide shoe assembly range includes the following specifications:

• Roller Ø80 × 25 × 6203
  • Roller Ø150 × 38 × 2-6003
  • Roller Ø200 × 30 × 6007
Note: Please verify the roller diameter, width and bearing specification before selecting a replacement roller guide shoe assembly.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator system, roller guide shoe assemblies help with:

• Guiding elevator cars along guide rails
  • Guiding counterweights during travel
  • Supporting alignment within the guide system
  • Assisting controlled vertical movement
  • Housing and supporting guide rollers
  • Contributing to guide shoe assembly operation
FAQ

1. What does a roller guide shoe assembly do?
It guides the elevator smoothly along the steel rails using rollers.

2. How often should rollers be replaced?
Replacement intervals can vary depending on the elevator system, operating conditions and maintenance requirements.

3. What are the signs of a failing roller guide?
Unusual noise, vibration or visible wear may indicate that guide system components require inspection.

4. Why choose roller guides over sliding shoes?
They minimize friction to save energy and provide a smoother ride.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Tachometer Roller

Tachometer rollers are commonly associated with older or specialised elevator installations where speed and distance information is required for system monitoring. Different roller specifications may be used depending on the elevator model and application requirements.
When replacing a tachometer roller, it is important to verify the existing specifications and system requirements to help identify a suitable replacement component.
Available KONE Tachometer Roller Options
The available KONE elevator tachometer roller range includes the following specifications:

• Tachometer Roller - Ø37.5 mm
  • Tachometer Roller - Ø75 mm
Note: Verify the roller diameter and existing component specifications before selecting a replacement roller.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator tachometer roller system, tachometer rollers are involved in:

• Transmitting movement-related information
  • Supporting speed monitoring functions
  • Supporting travel distance measurement
  • Working alongside elevator control systems
  • Assisting movement tracking applications
  • Contributing to elevator monitoring functions
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a KONE elevator tachometer roller?
A tachometer roller is used as part of a system that provides movement-related information for elevator monitoring and control functions.

2. What type of information does a tachometer roller help provide?
Depending on the system design, tachometer rollers may be associated with speed and travel distance monitoring functions.

3. What role does a tachometer roller play in elevator monitoring?
A tachometer roller works as part of a system that helps provide movement-related information used for elevator monitoring and control functions.

4. Why are tachometer rollers used in certain elevator systems?
Some elevator systems use tachometer rollers as part of their movement monitoring arrangement, depending on the design and control requirements of the installation.

paragraph_text: Kone Elevator Door Down Roller

A KONE elevator door down roller is a door system component used within selected elevator door assemblies. Installed as part of the door mechanism, it works alongside other door-related components during elevator door operation.

Different door systems may require specific roller dimensions and configurations depending on the elevator model and door assembly design. When selecting a replacement door down roller, it is important to verify the existing component specifications to help identify a suitable match.
Available KONE Door Down Roller Options
This category includes door down rollers in a variety of dimensional and mounting configurations to suit different KONE elevator door assemblies:

• Ø74 × 16 × M10
  • Ø64 × Ø39 × 16 × M10
  • Ø45 × 38 × 12 × M10
  • Ø70 × 17 × M10
  • Ø85 × 32 × 14 × M8
  • Ø50 × 42 × 12 × Ø10

  • Ø63 × 15 × M12
  • Ø70 × 17 × M8
  • Ø63 × 10.5 × M12
  • Ø80 × 11 × M8
Selection Tip: Compare the dimensions and mounting details of the existing component when identifying a suitable replacement.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator door system, door down rollers are involved in:

• Supporting door assembly movement
  • Assisting the operation of door components
  • Working alongside door roller arrangements
  • Supporting door system functionality
  • Contributing to door assembly operation
  • Assisting component movement within door mechanisms
FAQ

1. What is the function of the down roller?
It supports door panel weight and keeps doors perfectly aligned within the sills.

2. What happens if they are completely ignored?
The doors will start to jam or move unevenly. When this happens, the elevator will automatically shut down for safety, trapping people inside or leaving the elevator broken.

3. How do I know if they are broken or wearing out?
You will hear annoying squeaking, grinding or scraping noises whenever the elevator doors open and close.

4. Why do some door down rollers appear similar despite having different specifications?
Rollers can look similar visually while differing in dimensions or mounting arrangements.

groups.meta_title: Buy KONE Elevator Guide Shoe Insert | Lift Spare Parts
groups.meta_description: Explore KONE elevator guide shoe insert components used in sliding guide shoe assemblies for elevator car and counterweight applications.
groups.keywords: KONE Elevator Guide Shoe Inserts, KONE Guide Shoe Insert Parts, Elevator Guide Shoe Inserts UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Guide Shoe & Insert

Within a sliding guide shoe assembly, the KONE elevator guide shoe insert serves as the contact surface between the assembly and the guide rail. These inserts are used in selected KONE elevator systems and are available in different configurations depending on the application.

Unlike roller-based guidance arrangements, sliding guide shoes use inserts as part of their design. Because these inserts are separate components, they can be replaced when required without changing the complete guide shoe assembly. Different insert types may be used depending on the elevator model and guide shoe configuration.
Available KONE Guide Shoe Insert Options
The KONE elevator guide shoe insert category includes a range of insert and guide shoe specifications designed for different guide shoe assembly requirements:

• Guide Shoe Insert - 128 X 34 X 16mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 128 X 34 X 10mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 140 X 30 X 16mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 140 X 30 X 10mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 80 X 25 X 5mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 80 X 30 X 25 X 11mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 131 X 22 X 9mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 131 X 22 X 16mm
  • Guide Shoe Insert - 130 X 32 X 29 X 10/16mm
  • Guide Shoe With Rubber Cushion - 130 X 10/16mm
  • Guide Shoe - 120 X 22 X 29mm

Selection Reference: Guide shoe inserts and related components are available in different dimensions and configurations. Comparing the specifications of the existing component can help during product selection.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator system, guide shoe inserts are involved in:

• Providing a contact surface between the guide shoe and guide rail
  • Supporting sliding guide shoe arrangements
  • Working alongside guide rail systems
  • Forming part of elevator guidance assemblies
  • Assisting elevator car and counterweight travel
FAQ

1. How do I find out which size I currently have?
You can check the part number or measure the existing insert to help find the correct replacement.

2. Why should I buy just the insert instead of the whole guide shoe?
Because the guide shoe insert is a separate component within the guide shoe assembly. In many cases, the insert can be replaced without changing the entire assembly.

3. Can I use a 10mm insert on a 16mm rail?
No. Guide shoe inserts are available in different sizes and configurations. It is important to match the replacement insert with the specifications of the existing component.

4. What is its main role of a guide shoe insert?
A guide shoe insert sits between the guide shoe and guide rail and helps them work together during elevator travel.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Door Slider & Door Vane

KONE elevator door slider and door vane components are used within elevator door systems, with each component serving a different purpose during door operation. A door slider, also known as a door gib or door guide shoe, is typically installed at the bottom of the door panel and helps maintain door alignment and stability during movement.
A door vane is used as part of the car door operator assembly. It works alongside the door system to support the coordinated opening of the elevator car door and landing door.
At Bestech, door sliders and door vanes are available in a range of designs and specifications to suit elevator door system configurations.
Available KONE Door Slider & Door Vane Options
The KONE elevator door slider and door vane range includes components used in different door system arrangements:
Door Sliders

• Door Slider - 70 X 40 X 14
  • Door Slider - 70 X 42 X 12.5
  • Door Slider - 71 X 15 X 12
  • Door Slider - 71 X 14 X 3 - Φ7
  • Door Slider - 93 X 60 X 11
  • Door Slider - 61 X 68 X 12
Door Vanes

• Frequency Conversion Door Vane
  • Mechanical Door Vane
  • Door Vane
Product Note: Door sliders and door vanes are available in different dimensions and configurations depending on the elevator door system and application requirements.
Functions
KONE elevator door sliders and door vanes play a role in:

• Keeping door panels properly positioned
  • Supporting smooth door movement
  • Assisting door guidance and alignment
  • Supporting door opening operations
  • Working with other door system components
  • Contributing to overall door system operation
FAQ

1. Why are door sliders located at the bottom of elevator doors?
Door sliders are positioned within the door assembly to help maintain the movement and positioning of the door panel.

2. Do door sliders and door vanes perform the same function?
No. Each component has a different role within the elevator door system and operates alongside different door-related mechanisms.

3. What is the difference between a door slider and a door vane?
A door slider is associated with door guidance and positioning, while a door vane works as part of the door operating mechanism.

4. Why are door sliders and door vanes available in different designs?
Different elevator models and door systems may require specific component configurations.

faq.answer: A door slider is associated with door guidance and positioning, while a door vane works as part of the door operating mechanism.

groups.meta_title: Buy KONE Elevator Door Lock & Power Lock Components
groups.meta_description: Looking for KONE elevator door lock parts? Explore door lock and power lock options used in elevator landing door applications.
groups.keywords: KONE Elevator Door Lock, KONE Door Lock Spare Parts, Elevator Door Lock UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Door Lock & Power Lock

KONE elevator door locks are installed on landing doors and are used as part of the door locking system. They help manage when landing doors remain locked and when they can be opened during normal elevator operation.

Installed on each floor, these door lock assemblies operate as part of the landing door interlock arrangement. They work alongside other door components to support the opening, closing, locking and unlocking functions of elevator landing doors.
Available KONE Door Lock Options
The KONE elevator door lock range includes the following components:

• Door Lock (Right)
  • Power Lock - Ø28
  • Power Lock (Thin Type) - Ø28
Selection Tip: Existing part details and door lock configuration information can help when identifying a suitable replacement component.
Functions
The functions of KONE elevator door lock components include:

• Helps keep landing doors locked
  • Supports interlock operation
  • Assists door locking and unlocking functions
  • Supports coordinated door operation
  • Contributes to elevator door system performance
FAQ

1. What does the hallway door lock do?
It helps keep the landing door locked when the elevator car is not at that floor. When the elevator arrives, the lock works as part of the mechanism that allows the door to be opened.

2. What does the car power lock do?
It helps control the locking and unlocking of landing doors as part of the elevator door lock system.

3. How do I make sure I am buying the right hallway door lock?
The best way is to compare the existing part number, door lock type, and product specifications with the replacement component before ordering.

4. Is it safe for me to install these locks myself?
No, elevator door locks are critical safety devices and must always be installed by a certified elevator technician.

faq.answer: It helps control the locking and unlocking of landing doors as part of the elevator door lock system.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Sensors

Accurate floor levelling is an important part of elevator operation, as it help passengers enter and exit the elevator comfortably. KONE elevator sensors are used as part of the elevator control system and assist with monitoring elevator car position during travel.

Levelling sensors help the elevator determine when the car has reached the correct stopping position at a floor. By providing position-related information to the control system, these sensors support accurate floor levelling and contribute to smooth passenger movement between the elevator car and landing.
Available KONE Sensor Options

The available KONE levelling sensor range includes models such as 61U/61N, 77U/77N, BAR2000, 61U/61N/30S and 77U/77N/77M/77S, along with additional levelling sensor variants for selected elevator applications.
Functions
KONE elevator sensors help with:

• Monitoring elevator car position
  • Supporting accurate floor levelling
  • Assisting floor stop positioning
  • Providing information to the control system
  • Supporting elevator travel monitoring
  • Supporting elevator travel monitoring
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a KONE levelling sensor?
A levelling sensor helps the elevator determine the correct stopping position at a floor landing

2. Why is accurate floor levelling important?
Accurate levelling helps reduce the gap and height difference between the elevator car and the floor landing.

3. How does a levelling sensor work?
The sensor provides position-related information that is used by the elevator control system during travel and stopping operations.

4. Are levelling sensors used in every elevator?
Most elevator systems use sensors as part of their control and positioning arrangements, although the design may vary between models.

faq.answer: A levelling sensor helps the elevator determine the correct stopping position at a floor landing.

faq.answer: Accurate levelling helps reduce the gap and height difference between the elevator car and the floor landing.

faq.answer: The sensor provides position-related information that is used by the elevator control system during travel and stopping operations.

groups.meta_title: Elevator Inspection Box & Oil Box | KONE
groups.meta_description: Get essential components for elevator servicing and lubrication, including KONE inspection boxes and oil box solutions.
groups.keywords: KONE Inspection Box and Oil Box, KONE Elevator Inspection Box, Elevator Oil Box Parts UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Sensors

KONE elevator inspection oil and box components are used in different areas of the elevator system to support maintenance and operational functions. While oil boxes are associated with guide rail lubrication, inspection boxes allow technicians to operate and inspect the elevator during service procedures.
Oil boxes, also known as guide rail lubricators or oil cups, are used as small reservoirs that supply lubrication to guide rail systems.
Inspection boxes are typically installed on the roof of the elevator car and are used during maintenance, inspection and repair procedures.
Together, these components support different aspects of elevator servicing and system operation across a range of KONE elevator applications.
Available KONE Inspection Oil & Box Options
The current KONE inspection oil and box range includes:

• Car Top Inspection Box (KOIB01) - used for elevator inspection and service operations.
  • Oil Box Square (KOOC01) - used as part of guide rail lubrication arrangements.
Functions
KONE inspection oil and box components help with:

• Supporting guide rail lubrication
  • Holding lubricant for guide rail applications
  • Assisting elevator maintenance activities
  • Providing access to inspection controls
  • Supporting service and repair procedures
  • Working alongside elevator maintenance systems
FAQ

1. What is an oil box in a KONE elevator?
An oil box, also known as a guide rail lubricator or oil cup, is used to hold lubricant for guide rail applications.

2. What is a car top inspection box?
A car top inspection box is a control station installed on the roof of the elevator car and used during inspection and maintenance activities.

3. Why are oil boxes used in elevator systems?
Oil boxes are used to store lubricant for selected guide rail applications.

4. Do oil boxes and inspection boxes serve the same purpose?
No. Oil boxes are associated with lubrication, while inspection boxes are associated with maintenance and inspection functions.

groups.alttext_group: KONE Elevator Spare Parts Service Tool & Decoder
groups.meta_title: KONE Elevator Service Tool Decoder | Decode Boards
groups.meta_description: Discover KONE service tools and decode boards that support elevator monitoring, diagnostics and information exchange functions.
groups.keywords: KONE Elevator Service Tools and Decoders, KONE Service Tool Parts, Elevator Service Tools UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Service Tool & Decoder

KONE elevator service tool and decoder components are used within elevator control systems as part of communication and interface arrangements. Decode boards are electronic components used within elevator control systems and are designed to work alongside other control system components.
As part of the control architecture, decode boards are associated with the exchange of information between connected system components.
Service tools are used by technicians when carrying out maintenance, inspection, and troubleshooting activities.
Available KONE Service Tool & Decoder Options
This category includes the following KONE elevator control system components:

• Service Tool (KOTT01)
  • Decode Board (KOTT02)
Product Note: While the service tool is used for elevator diagnostics and service-related activities, the decode board functions as part of the control system communication arrangement.
Functions
Within a KONE elevator system, these components help with:
Service Tool

• Supports elevator diagnostic activities
  • Assists maintenance and inspection procedures
  • Helps technicians perform system checks
  • Supports troubleshooting activities
Decode Board

• Supports communication between control system components
  • Assists information exchange within the control system
  • Functions as an interface module
  • Supports control system operation
FAQ

1. What is a decode board?
A decode board is a PCB that functions as an interface or communication module within the elevator control system.

2. What is a KONE service tool?
A KONE service tool is used by technicians during elevator maintenance, diagnostics and service procedures.

3. What happens if a KONE decoder board stops working?
If it stops working, the affected components may no longer communicate properly, which can interfere with the operation of the related elevator functions.

4. Where is a decoder board used?
A decoder board is used inside the elevator control system, where it acts as a link between electronic components that need to exchange information with one another.

5. Is a decoder board the same as a main control board?
No, the main control board acts as the master brain of the lift, while the decoder board acts as a helper module that processes communication lines.

groups.meta_description: Encoder components used in KONE elevator systems for drive feedback, travel monitoring and position-related functions.
groups.keywords: KONE Elevator Encoders, KONE Encoder Modules, Elevator Encoder Parts UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Encoder

A KONE elevator encoder is an electronic feedback device used within the elevator drive and control system. It monitors elevator movement and provides position-related information that helps the system manage elevator travel.

Encoders play an important role in motion control by supplying feedback to the drive system during operation. This information helps support accurate elevator movement, floor levelling and overall system performance.
Available KONE Encoder Options
Models include:

ApplicationOEM Number
KDL16L InverterKM950278G01
MX14 MotorKM1331189
MX14 Motor, TS5246N705KM5002777
Note: These encoder models are designed for specific elevator drive and motor applications and may vary depending on the system configuration.
Functions
KONE elevator encoders play a role in:

• Providing feedback to the drive system
  • Supporting precise motion control
  • Assisting accurate elevator positioning
  • Supporting floor levelling functions
  • Monitoring elevator movement
  • Contributing to elevator operation
FAQ

1. What should be checked before selecting a replacement encoder?
The encoder model, OEM number and associated motor or drive system details should be matched with the existing component.

2. Why is encoder feedback important?
Encoder feedback helps the drive system manage elevator movement and positioning during operation.

3. Does an elevator encoder measure speed or position?
An elevator encoder provides movement-related feedback that can be used by the control system to monitor position and movement characteristics during operation.

4. Why do encoder models have different OEM numbers?
OEM numbers help identify specific encoder models and applications, making it easier to match replacement components with the existing system.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator Door Motor & Controller

KONE elevator door motor and controller components are used within elevator door systems to support door operation. Door motors provide the driving force required to power the car door operator and move the elevator car doors. Door controller boards are dedicated electronic PCBs that manage the functions of the car door operator and coordinate door movement during operation.
The category also includes related door operator components such as door motor transformers used in selected elevator door systems.
Working together within the door operator system, these components help support smooth and controlled door opening and closing functions.

A door motor provides the power required to operate the elevator car door system.

No. The door motor provides movement, while the controller board manages and controls door operation.

Modern elevator door systems typically use dedicated control components to manage door operation, although designs may vary between models and manufacturers.

KONE elevator frequency inverters are core electronic drive components used to control the operation of the elevator traction motor. Also known as Variable Voltage Variable Frequency (VVVF) drives or traction drive units, these devices regulate motor speed, torque and direction during elevator travel.
As a key part of the elevator drive system, frequency inverters provide the control required for elevator acceleration, deceleration and movement management.

  • Assisting elevator movement control

A frequency inverter is an electronic drive unit used to control the operation of the elevator traction motor.

2. What does a VVVF drive do in an elevator?
A VVVF drive regulates motor speed, torque and direction to support elevator movement.

3. Why is a frequency inverter important?
The frequency inverter provides the control needed for motor operation and elevator travel management.

groups.keywords: KONE Elevator HOP, KONE Hall Operating Panel, Elevator HOP Panels UAE
paragraph_text: KONE Elevator HOP

A KONE elevator HOP (Hall Operating Panel) is installed outside the elevator car at each landing floor and is used by passengers to place elevator calls. Working together with the COP (Car Operating Panel) located inside the elevator car, the HOP forms part of the passenger interface system used during elevator operation.
Available KONE Elevator HOP Options

Bestech offers a range of KONE elevator HOP (Hall Operating Panel) variants for selected elevator systems. Available products within this category can be referenced using the following internal identification codes:

  • KOHOP05
  • KOHOP06
Product Note: The codes listed above are internal product references. HOP designs and configurations may vary depending on the elevator system and application.
Functions
KONE elevator HOPs help with:

• Registering elevator call requests
  • Supporting passenger interaction at landing floors
  • Working alongside the COP system
  • Assisting passenger access to elevator services
  • Contributing to elevator system operation
FAQ

1. What does HOP stand for in an elevator?
HOP stands for Hall Operating Panel, which is typically installed outside the elevator car on each floor.

2. What is the difference between a HOP and a COP?
A HOP is located at the landing floor and is used to call the elevator, while a COP is located inside the elevator car and is used for floor selection and other passenger controls.

3. Does every floor have a HOP?
The number and configuration of HOP units depend on the elevator system and building design.

4. What is the purpose of a KONE elevator HOP?
A KONE elevator HOP is used to place elevator call requests from a landing floor and forms part of the passenger interface system.

paragraph_text: KONE Elevator PC Board

KONE elevator PC boards (Printed Circuit Boards) are electronic control components used within elevator control and monitoring systems. As part of KONE's modular control architecture, these boards are used across different control, communication, safety and operational functions within the elevator system.

KONE control systems commonly utilise Lift Control Electronics (LCE) and other related electronic assemblies, with different PC board types designed for specific applications. Depending on the system configuration, PC boards may be used for control processing, communication, safety monitoring and other elevator functions.
Available Product Options

Additional PC Board Variants

The KONE elevator PC board range also includes additional OEM-specific PC board variants for selected applications, including models such as KM713730G01, KM7477652G01, KM166628G04, KM713180G01, KM825950G01 and other board references used within KONE elevator control systems.
Functions
KONE elevator PC boards help with:

• Supporting elevator control functions
  • Assisting communication between system components
  • Supporting signal processing operations
  • Assisting safety monitoring functions
  • Supporting elevator drive and control systems
  • Contributing to overall elevator system operation
FAQ

1. What is a KONE elevator PC board?
A KONE elevator PC board is an electronic circuit board used within the elevator control system to support specific operational, control or communication functions.

2. What does PCB stand for in an elevator?
PCB stands for Printed Circuit Board, an electronic component used within elevator control and monitoring systems.

3. What is LCE in a KONE elevator?
LCE stands for Lift Control Electronics, a control architecture used in KONE elevator systems.

4. Are all KONE PC boards used for the same purpose?
No. Different PC boards are designed for specific functions such as control processing, communication, safety monitoring or drive system operation.

faq.question: What does PCB stand for in an elevator?
faq.answer: PCB stands for Printed Circuit Board, an electronic component used within elevator control and monitoring systems.

groups.alttext_group: MITSUBISHI Elevator Spare Parts Push Buttons
groups.meta_title: Mitsubishi Elevator Push Buttons | Spare Parts
groups.meta_description: Discover Mitsubishi elevator push button solutions for hall stations, car operating panels and elevator control interfaces.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Push Buttons, Mitsubishi Elevator Button Parts
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Push Buttons

Mitsubishi elevator push buttons serve as the primary interface between passengers and the elevator control system. Installed within elevator cars and at landing stations, these buttons allow passengers to place elevator calls and select travel destinations during operation.

Mitsubishi uses different push button designs and configurations depending on the elevator system generation and the specific application, including hall stations and car operating panels. Push buttons may vary in size, mounting specifications and operating features across different elevator models.
Available Mitsubishi Elevator Push Button Options
HOPE Type Push Buttons

• Push Button HOPE Type – Installation Hole Size: 31 × 31
  • Push Button HOPE Type – Installation Hole Size: 38.5 × 32.5
Standard Push Buttons

• Push Button – Installation Hole Size: Φ32.5
  • Push Button – Installation Hole Size: 39 × 33
  • Push Button – Installation Hole Size: Φ33
LEHY-11 Push Buttons

• Push Button LEHY-11 – Installation Hole Size: Φ35
  • Push Button LEHY-11 – Installation Hole Size: Φ37
Model-Specific Push Buttons
Available models include LHB-005B, LHB-007B, LHB-006B, LHB-051A, LHB-008B, LHB-052A, J-M5112, D51.31AG01 and J-M5497-01.
Functions
Mitsubishi elevator push buttons help with:

• Registering passenger requests
  • Sending call inputs to the elevator system
  • Supporting floor selection functions
  • Used in hall stations and car operating panels
  • Assisting passenger interaction with the elevator system
  • Contributing to elevator operation
FAQ

1. What is a Mitsubishi elevator push button?
A Mitsubishi elevator push button is a passenger interface component used to register elevator calls and floor selection requests.

2. Where are elevator push buttons installed?
Push buttons may be installed in hall stations outside the elevator and within the car operating panel inside the elevator car.

3. Why are different push button sizes available?
Different elevator models and panel designs may require specific button dimensions and mounting configurations.

4. What is the difference between hall push buttons and car push buttons?
Hall push buttons are used to call the elevator from a floor landing, while car push buttons are used inside the elevator car for floor selection.

groups.meta_title: Buy Mitsubishi Elevator Rollers & Sheaves | Bestech UAE
groups.meta_description: A range of Mitsubishi elevator door rollers, belt rollers and sheaves designed for selected elevator door and traction system applications.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator door rollers, Mitsubishi Elevator belt roller, Mitsubishi Elevator sheaves
paragraph_text: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Rollers, Belt Roller & Sheaves

Mitsubishi elevator door rollers, belt rollers, lock rollers, door vane rollers and sheaves are used in different areas of the elevator system. Each component performs a specific role in supporting door operation, traction functions and the interaction of elevator system components.
1. Door Rollers

Door rollers are key components of the elevator door system and are used to support the movement of both car doors and landing doors. Installed within the door mechanism, these rollers help guide the door panels during opening and closing operations. Different roller sizes and configurations may be used depending on the door system design.
Available product options include:

• Door Roller Nylon (Φ56 × 14 × 6201 / Φ72 × 15 × 6202)
  • Door Roller (Φ56 × 14 × 6201 / Φ25 × 10 × M8 / Φ72 × 15 × 6202)
  • Door Roller With Axle (Φ56 × 14 × M10)
  • Door Down Roller With Axle (Φ72 × 15 × M10)
  • Door Down Roller (Φ40 × 12 × M8 / Φ40 × 14 × M12 / Φ26 × 9.5 × M8)
  • Car Door Roller (Φ84 × 18 × 6204)
2. Belt Rollers

Mitsubishi elevator belt rollers are associated with belt-driven traction systems and work alongside elevator belts during operation. They are used in selected elevator designs where belts form part of the traction arrangement and help support belt guidance within the system.
Available product options include:

• Belt Roller Φ90 × 30 × Φ20 × 32
  • Belt Roller Φ90 × 27 × 6201
3. Lock Rollers

Lock rollers are used within landing door lock assemblies and form part of the door interlock mechanism. They work alongside other door lock components and are associated with the interaction between the moving elevator car door and the landing door locking arrangement.
Available product options include:

• Lock Roller Φ46 × 13 × (6002) Φ15
  • Lock Roller Φ60 × 16 × Φ15
4. Door Vane Rollers

Door vane rollers are associated with the car door vane or clutch assembly. These components work with other door system parts during door operation and support the engagement process between the car door mechanism and the landing door system.
Available product options include:
• Door Vane Roller Φ46 × 13 × Φ12
5. Sheaves

Mitsubishi elevator sheaves are wheel-shaped components used within elevator traction systems. They work with elevator ropes or belts and form part of the mechanism that transfers movement from the drive system through the traction arrangement during elevator operation.
Available product options include:

• Landing Door Linkage Sheave Φ90 × 12 × 6200
  • Sheave Components Φ90 × 12 × 6200
  • Door Sheave Φ90 × 12 × 6200
Additional Roller Components

• Guide Shoe Roller Φ150 × 32 × 2-6203
  • Roller Guide Shoe Assembly, Roller Φ195 × 38 × 2-6003, Mounting Hole Φ140 × 70
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator rollers and sheaves are available in different dimensions and configurations depending on the elevator model, door system and traction system requirements.
Functions

ComponentsFunctions
Door Rollers
  1. 1. Supporting the movement of car and landing door panels
  2. 2. Assisting door guidance during opening and closing
  3. 3. Working as part of the elevator door system
Belt Rollers
  1. 1. Supporting belt guidance applications
  2. 2. Working alongside elevator belt systems
  3. 3. Assisting traction system operation
Lock Rollers
  1. 1. Supporting landing door interlock arrangements
  2. 2. Assisting interaction between door lock components
  3. 3. Working as part of the door locking mechanism
Door Vane Rollers
  1. 1. Supporting door clutch or vane assemblies
  2. 2. Assisting door engagement functions
  3. 3. Working alongside door system components
Sheaves
  1. 1. Working with elevator ropes or belts
  2. 2. Supporting traction system operation
  3. 3. Assisting elevator movement functions
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a Mitsubishi elevator door roller?
A Mitsubishi elevator door roller is used within the elevator door system to support the movement and guidance of car and landing door panels during opening and closing operations.

2. What does a belt roller do in an elevator?
A belt roller is used in selected belt-driven elevator systems and works alongside elevator belts as part of the traction arrangement.

3. What is the difference between a door roller and a lock roller?
A door roller is associated with the movement of elevator door panels, while a lock roller forms part of the landing door lock assembly and works with door interlock components.

4. What is a door vane roller used for?
A door vane roller is associated with the car door vane or clutch assembly and supports the engagement process between door system components during operation.

5. What is a sheave in a Mitsubishi elevator?
A sheave is a wheel-shaped component used within the elevator traction system and works with elevator ropes or belts during elevator operation.

groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Guide Shoes, Mitsubishi Guide Shoe Inserts, Elevator Guide Shoe Assemblies UAE
paragraph_text: Mitsubishi Elevator Guide Shoes

Mitsubishi elevator guide shoes are components used within the elevator guidance system and work alongside guide rails during elevator operation. The category includes guide shoe inserts, guide shoe assemblies and guide shoe rollers designed for selected Mitsubishi elevator applications.
Guide shoe components are available in different sizes and configurations depending on the elevator model and system requirements.
1. Mitsubishi Guide Shoe Inserts

Mitsubishi guide shoe inserts are installed within guide shoe assemblies and work alongside elevator guide rails during operation. Different insert dimensions are available to suit various guide shoe configurations.
Available product options include:

Guide Shoe Insert (One 230 × 35 × 8, Two 230 × 30 × 12.5), Guide Shoe Insert (One 230 × 35 × 9, Two 230 × 30 × 9), Guide Shoe Insert (120 × 16), Guide Shoe Insert (120 × 10), Guide Shoe Insert (120 × 27 × 9) and Head of Guide Shoe Insert (86 × 18).
2. Mitsubishi Guide Shoe Assemblies
Mitsubishi elevator guide shoe assemblies are used as part of the elevator guidance arrangement and are available in different dimensions and mounting configurations for selected elevator systems.
Available product options include:

Guide Shoe Assembly (86 × 18/12), Guide Shoe Assembly (230 × 10/16), Guide Shoe Assembly (86 × 18/12, Mounting Hole: 68), Guide Shoe Assembly (230 × 10/16, Mounting Hole: 150 × 100), Guide Shoe Assembly (120 × 10/16, Mounting Hole: 90) and Roller Guide Shoe Assembly (Roller: Φ195 × 38 × 2-6003, Mounting Hole: Φ140 × 70).
3. Mitsubishi Guide Shoe Rollers
Mitsubishi guide shoe rollers are used within roller-type guide shoe arrangements and are available in different roller dimensions for selected elevator applications.
Available product options include:
Guide Shoe Roller (Φ80 × 26.5 × 6203), Guide Shoe Roller (Φ260 × 32 × Φ52), Guide Shoe Roller (Φ150 × 32 × 2-6203) and Guide Shoe Roller (Φ195 × 38 × 2-6003).
Functions

ComponentsFunctions
Guide Shoe InsertsInstalled within guide shoe assemblies and form part of the guide shoe contact arrangement.
Guide Shoe AssembliesUsed as a complete guide shoe unit and provide the housing for guide shoe inserts or rollers.
Guide Shoe RollersUsed in roller-type guide shoe systems and work with guide rails during elevator operation.
FAQ

1. What is a Mitsubishi elevator guide shoe?
A Mitsubishi elevator guide shoe is a component used within the elevator guidance system and works alongside the guide rails during operation.

2. What is the purpose of a guide shoe insert?
A guide shoe insert is installed within a guide shoe assembly and forms part of the guide shoe arrangement used in the elevator system.

3. What is the difference between a guide shoe assembly and a guide shoe insert?
A guide shoe assembly is the complete guide shoe unit, while the guide shoe insert is a component used within the assembly.

4. What are guide shoe rollers used for?
Guide shoe rollers are used in roller-type guide shoe arrangements and work alongside elevator guide rails during operation.

groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Hanger, Mitsubishi Door Hanger Parts, Elevator Door Hanger UAE
paragraph_text: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Hanger

Mitsubishi elevator door hangers are structural door system components used to support elevator door panels and related door operation mechanisms. Depending on the application, door hanger assemblies may be used for elevator car doors or landing doors within the hoistway.

Landing door hangers are installed above the landing door opening and support the weight of the landing door panel assembly. These components form part of the door system arrangement used at each floor landing. Mitsubishi door hanger assemblies are available in different configurations depending on the door type, opening width and locking arrangement.
Available Mitsubishi Door Hanger Parts
The Mitsubishi elevator door hanger range includes car door hanger and landing door hanger assemblies for selected elevator applications.
Car Door Hangers

• Car Door Hanger, JJ = 700–1100
  • Car Door Hanger, JJ = 1100–1800
  • Car Door Hanger With 161A Door Lock, JJ = 1100–1800
Landing Door Hangers

• Landing Door Hanger With 161 Door Lock, JJ = 1100–1800
  • Landing Door Hanger With Sheave, JJ = 700–1100
  • Landing Door Hanger With Hook Lock, JJ = 700–1100
Functions
Mitsubishi elevator door hangers help with:

• Supporting elevator door panel assemblies
  • Supporting door movement arrangements
  • Working with door locking mechanisms
  • Supporting landing door and car door applications
  • Forming part of the elevator door system structure
FAQ

1. What is a Mitsubishi elevator door hanger?
A Mitsubishi elevator door hanger is a structural component used to support elevator door panels and related door system mechanisms.

2. What is the difference between a car door hanger and a landing door hanger?
A car door hanger is associated with the elevator car door system, while a landing door hanger is installed at the floor landing and supports the landing door assembly.

3. Where is a landing door hanger installed?
A landing door hanger is installed above the landing door opening and forms part of the door system arrangement at each floor.

4. What is the purpose of a door lock within a door hanger assembly?
A door lock is used as part of the elevator door system and helps keep the landing door secured when the elevator is not at that floor.

groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Sliders, Mitsubishi Door Slider Parts, Elevator Door Sliders UAE
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Door Sliders

Mitsubishi elevator door sliders are also known as door gibs or door guide shoes and form part of the door guidance system used in elevator door assemblies. Installed at the lower portion of the door, they work alongside guide arrangements to support door movement during opening and closing operations.
Mitsubishi door slider parts are available in different dimensions to suit various door system configurations and elevator applications.
Applications
Mitsubishi elevator door sliders are commonly used in:

• Landing door guidance arrangements
  • Door guide shoe systems
  • Door gib applications
  • Elevator door movement systems
  • Selected Mitsubishi elevator door assemblies
Available Mitsubishi Door Slider Parts
The Mitsubishi elevator door slider range includes:

• Door Slider 65 × 40 × 11.5
  • Door Slider 62 × 40 × 10
  • Door Slider 54 × 44 × 12
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator door sliders are available in different sizes and configurations depending on the elevator door system requirements.
Functions

Mitsubishi elevator door sliders help guide the bottom edge of the door as it moves within the door system. Working as part of the door guidance arrangement, they support the alignment and movement path of the door during operation.
FAQ

1. What is a Mitsubishi elevator door slider?
A Mitsubishi elevator door slider is a component located at the bottom of the elevator door that works with the guide arrangement to support door movement during opening and closing.

2. Can the same door slider be used for all Mitsubishi elevators?
No. Door slider dimensions and configurations may vary depending on the elevator model and door system requirements.

3. Is a door slider the same as a door gib?
Yes. In many elevator systems, a door slider may also be referred to as a door gib or door guide shoe depending on the manufacturer and application.

4. What is the purpose of a door slider?
A door slider works alongside the door guide system and supports the movement and guidance of the door during operation.

paragraph_text: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Vanes

Mitsubishi elevator door vanes are mechanical components used within the car door operator assembly. Installed as part of the elevator door mechanism, they work alongside other door system components during door operation.

The primary role of a door vane is to transmit motion from the moving car door to the landing door arrangement, allowing the door systems to operate together during opening and closing cycles. Mitsubishi door vane parts are available in different configurations depending on the elevator model and door system design.
Available Mitsubishi Door Vane Parts
The Mitsubishi elevator door vane range includes:

• Door Vane 161 (PB255)
  • Door Vane SLK161-06 (PB256)
  • Door Vane HA1410A1
  • SLH161-08 Door Vane
Note: Mitsubishi elevator door vanes are available in different configurations depending on the door operator system and application requirements.
Function of the Door Vanes
Mitsubishi elevator door vanes form part of the car door operator assembly and are associated with the transfer of motion between the car door and landing door systems during operation.
FAQ

1. How does a door vane work in an elevator system?
A door vane works as part of the car door operator assembly and is associated with the interaction between the elevator car door and the landing door system during operation.

2. Is a door vane used on the car door or the landing door?
A door vane is typically associated with the elevator car door assembly and works alongside components involved in landing door operation.

3. What should be checked before selecting a replacement door vane?
The existing door vane model, dimensions and elevator system specifications should be verified before selecting a replacement component.

4. Are door vanes part of the door operator assembly?
Yes. Door vanes are associated with the door operating mechanism and work alongside other door system components during operation.

paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Door Locks, Triangle Locks & Power Locks

Mitsubishi elevator door lock components are used within elevator door safety and access systems. The category includes door locks, triangle locks, triangle lock keys and power lock components designed for selected Mitsubishi elevator applications.
Different lock types perform different functions within the elevator system, ranging from landing door locking arrangements to authorised door access mechanisms.
1. Mitsubishi Elevator Door Locks

Mitsubishi elevator door locks are associated with the landing door interlock assembly installed at each floor landing. These components form part of the door locking arrangement and work alongside elevator safety systems during operation.
Available Product Options Include:

• Door Lock PB161A
  • Door Lock (Left) (PB259)
  • Door Lock Part
  • Door Lock, 161
  • Door Lock, Small SK-C
  • Door Lock, Small SK-A
  • Door Lock Contact, 161
  • Door Lock Contact 161A-13
  • Door Lock Contact PB161A-1
2. Mitsubishi Triangle Locks

Mitsubishi triangle locks are specialised door access mechanisms associated with landing door access arrangements. These components are designed for use with authorised unlocking procedures and related elevator door systems.
Available Product Options Include:
• Triangle Lock PB263
3. Mitsubishi Triangle Lock Keys
Mitsubishi triangle lock keys are specialised tools used with compatible triangle lock mechanisms. These components are intended for authorised access to elevator landing door systems.
Available Product Options Include:
• Triangle Lock Key
4. Mitsubishi Power Locks

Mitsubishi power locks are associated with elevator door locking arrangements that incorporate electrical verification as part of the door interlock system. These components are available in different configurations for selected elevator applications.
Available Product Options Include:

• Power Lock For HOP Φ20
  • Power Lock For COP
  • Power Lock, 30 × 30
Functions

ComponentsFunctions
Door LocksForm part of the landing door interlock arrangement.
Triangle LocksSupport authorised access to landing door systems.
Triangle Lock KeysUsed with compatible triangle lock mechanisms.
Power LocksAssociated with door locking arrangements incorporating electrical verification.
FAQ

1. What is the difference between a door lock and a power lock?
A door lock is associated with the mechanical locking arrangement of the landing door, while a power lock incorporates electrical verification as part of the interlock system.

2. Why are door lock contacts listed separately from door locks?
Door lock contacts are associated with the electrical portion of the door interlock arrangement, whereas the door lock assembly includes the mechanical locking components.

3. Can door lock configurations vary between Mitsubishi elevator models?
Yes. Door lock designs, contact arrangements and locking configurations may vary depending on the elevator model and door system requirements.

4. What is the purpose of a door interlock system?
The door interlock system forms part of the elevator safety arrangement and is associated with the operation of landing door locking mechanisms.

5. Are triangle locks and door locks the same component?
No. A door lock forms part of the landing door interlock system, while a triangle lock is an access mechanism used to operate the door unlocking arrangement.

paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Sensors

Mitsubishi elevators utilise a variety of sensors to support safety, precision and efficient system operation. These devices are designed to detect physical conditions such as presence, position, speed or force and convert them into electrical signals that can be processed by the elevator control system.

Different sensor types are used throughout Mitsubishi elevator systems depending on the monitoring and operational requirements of the application. The Mitsubishi elevator sensor range includes levelling sensors, magnetic sensors and other sensor components available in different configurations for selected elevator systems.
Available Mitsubishi Elevator Sensor Parts
Levelling Sensors

• Levelling Sensor YG-25
  • Levelling Sensor PAD-1
Magnetic Sensors

• Magnetic Sensor ADS-51M
  • Magnetic Sensor KCB-III
  • Magnetic Sensor YG-29
  • Magnetic Sensor YG-1A
  • Magnetic Sensor TNG-DS26E4
  • Magnetic Sensor YG-28
  • Magnetic Sensor YG-30
  • Magnetic Sensor YG-20
  • Magnetic Sensor PSMO-25G1 (YX40C1097-01A)
Sensor Components

• Sensor YX302C481-01 (X54HA-01)
  • Sensor HC-MT100V4812 (YX-302C031-02)
  • Sensor ESD-R-38B
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator sensors are available in different types and specifications depending on the sensing application and elevator system requirements.
Functions

Mitsubishi elevator sensors provide information used by the elevator control system to monitor operating conditions and respond to system inputs. Depending on the sensor type, these components may be associated with positioning, levelling, detection and other elevator monitoring functions.
FAQ

1. Why are different types of sensors used in Mitsubishi elevators?
Different sensors are designed to detect specific operating conditions, depending on the monitoring and control requirements of the elevator system.

2. What is the difference between a levelling sensor and a magnetic sensor?
Levelling sensors are associated with elevator positioning functions, while magnetic sensors are used in applications that require magnetic field detection.

3. Are all Mitsubishi elevator sensors used for the same purpose?
No. Different sensors are designed to detect different operating conditions and perform specific monitoring functions within the elevator system.

4. How do elevator sensors communicate with the control system?
Elevator sensors detect specific operating conditions and convert them into electrical signals that can be processed by the elevator controller.

paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Oil & Inspection Box

Mitsubishi elevator oil boxes, oil cans, oil seals and inspection boxes support different lubrication, sealing and maintenance requirements within elevator systems. These components are used in various parts of the elevator system depending on their specific application and operational requirements.

Oil boxes, often referred to as oil cups or guide rail lubricators, are used as lubricant reservoirs within guide rail lubrication arrangements. They are associated with maintaining lubrication along elevator guide rail systems.
Oil cans are also associated with guide rail lubrication applications and are used as part of lubrication arrangements designed to supply lubricant to elevator guide rails during operation.

Oil seals are used in applications where lubricants need to be retained and protected from contamination. Different oil seal configurations may be used depending on the elevator machine design and application requirements.
Car top inspection boxes are installed on the elevator car and form part of the inspection and maintenance arrangement used during servicing and inspection procedures.
Available Product Options
1. Mitsubishi Oil Boxes & Oil Cans

• Oil Box Square
  • Oil Box Round
  • Oil Can
  • Oil Box Felt
2. Mitsubishi Oil Seals

• Oil Seal, Φ78 × Φ55 × 12
  • Oil Seal, Φ95 × Φ70 × 12
3. Mitsubishi Car Top Inspection Box
• Car Top Inspection Box, 210 × 72
Functions

Mitsubishi elevator oil boxes and oil cans are associated with guide rail lubrication arrangements, while oil seals are used in applications involving lubricant retention and contamination control. Car top inspection boxes form part of elevator inspection and maintenance arrangements.
FAQ

1. What is the difference between an oil box and an oil seal?
An oil box is associated with guide rail lubrication, while an oil seal is used to help retain lubricants and limit contamination within specific elevator components.

2. What is the purpose of oil box felt?
Oil box felt is associated with guide rail lubrication arrangements and works alongside oil box components within the lubricator assembly.

3. What is a car top inspection box used for?
A car top inspection box is associated with elevator inspection and maintenance activities and forms part of the service arrangement installed on the elevator car.

4. How can I identify the correct lubrication component for my elevator system?
The existing component type, dimensions and elevator model information should be checked before selecting a replacement part.

faq.answer: An oil box is associated with guide rail lubrication, while an oil seal is used to help retain lubricants and limit contamination within specific elevator components.

faq.question: What is the purpose of oil box felt?
faq.answer: Oil box felt is associated with guide rail lubrication arrangements and works alongside oil box components within the lubricator assembly.

faq.question: What is a car top inspection box used for?
faq.answer: A car top inspection box is associated with elevator inspection and maintenance activities and forms part of the service arrangement installed on the elevator car.

faq.answer: The existing component type, dimensions and elevator model information should be checked before selecting a replacement part.

groups.alttext_group: MITSUBISHI Elevator Spare Parts Encoders & Modules
groups.meta_title: Buy Mitsubishi Elevator Encoders and Modules | Bestech UAE
groups.meta_description: A range of Mitsubishi encoder and module components designed for elevator monitoring, control and system operation.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Encoders and Modules, Mitsubishi Encoder Module Parts, Elevator Encoder Modules UAE
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Encoders & Modules

Mitsubishi elevator encoders and modules are important electronic components used within elevator control and drive systems. These components support monitoring, control and operational functions that contribute to the performance of the elevator system.

Encoders are high-precision sensing devices that provide continuous feedback on elevator position, speed and direction. This information is used by the control system to support accurate elevator movement and positioning during operation.

Modules are self-contained electronic or power components designed for specific system functions. Mitsubishi elevator systems utilise different module types to support control, power and operational requirements, while also simplifying maintenance and component replacement.
Available Product Options
Encoders

• ZKT-18B
  • ZKT-18B-51.2B-G12C (3.5M) – Z65AC-08
  • X65AC-20
  • X65AC-01
  • X65AC-10
  • SBX-8192-5MD (X65AC-31)
  • YTJ031-14
  • SBU-8192-6MD (Z65AC-003)
  • SBH-8192-5MD
  • ZKT-18B-51.2G12C (Z65AC-012)
  • ZKT-18B-51.2B-G12C-3550MM (Z65AC-011)
  • 47HB1321ZD50H8-B-35-1 (Z65AC-018)
  • 15BBRX-1019-C05
Modules

• 7MBP150RA120-05
  • CM75E3Y-24E
  • CM150DY-24H
  • QM75E1Y-H
  • QM300HH-H
  • CM150DY-24NF
  • PM100RSE120
  • PM150RSE120
  • 2MBI150NC-120
  • CM600HA-24H
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator encoders and modules are available in different specifications and configurations depending on the elevator system and application requirements.
Functions
Encoders provide feedback information related to elevator position, speed and direction, supporting elevator monitoring and control functions.
Modules support specific electronic or power-related functions within the elevator system and form part of the overall control and operational arrangement.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of an encoder in a Mitsubishi elevator?
Encoders provide continuous movement feedback that helps the elevator control system support smooth operation and accurate positioning.

2. Why is position feedback important in an elevator system?
Position feedback helps the elevator control system monitor movement and support accurate positioning during operation.

3. What is a module in a Mitsubishi elevator system?
A module is a self-contained electronic or power component designed to perform a specific function within the elevator system.

4. How do encoders and the control system work together?
Encoders provide feedback signals that the elevator control system uses to monitor and respond to elevator movement during operation.

groups.alttext_group: MITSUBISHI Elevator Spare Parts Door Motors,Fan & Brakes
groups.meta_title: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Motors, Fans & Brakes | UAE
groups.meta_description: Mitsubishi elevator door motors, brake components and fan parts available for selected elevator systems and replacement requirements.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Door Motors and Brakes, Mitsubishi Elevator Fan Motors, Elevator Door Motor Parts UAE
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Door Motors, Fan & Brakes

Door operation, braking performance and elevator ventilation rely on a range of specialised components within Mitsubishi elevator systems. This category includes door motors, brake components and fan units designed for selected elevator applications.
Door motors are specialised electric motors used within the car door operator assembly. They provide the controlled movement required for the opening and closing of elevator doors during operation.

Brakes are associated with elevator traction systems and are designed to support stopping and holding functions when elevator movement is not required. Brake-related components may include brake assemblies and supporting parts used within the braking arrangement.
Fan motors and fan components are used in elevator ventilation applications and are associated with air circulation within the elevator car depending on the system configuration.
Available Product Options
Door Motors

• Door Motor EMB-80-4
  • Door Motor EMB-48-8
  • Door Motor (YTJ031-14)
Brake Components

• Magnetic Brake (P101041A140G01L01)
  • Brake Plunger
  • Brake Copper Bush, Size: 60 × 80 × 55
Fan Components

• Fan, Mounting Hole: 380 × 90, AC: 220V (GYQF-330)
  • Fan, Mounting Hole: 380 × 90, AC: 220V (FB-9B)
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator motors, brakes and fan components are available in different specifications depending on the elevator model and application requirements.
Functions
Door Motors: Support the controlled opening and closing of elevator doors as part of the car door operator assembly.
Brakes: Support stopping and holding functions within the elevator traction system when movement is not required.
Fan Components: Support air circulation and ventilation arrangements within elevator applications.
FAQ

1. What is the function of a Mitsubishi elevator door motor?
It provides the controlled movement required for opening and closing the elevator doors during operation.

2. Why is the brake important in an elevator system?
The brake is associated with stopping and holding functions that help maintain the elevator in a stationary position when movement is not required.

3. What is a brake plunger used for?
A brake plunger is used as part of the elevator braking arrangement and is associated with the operation of the brake mechanism during stopping and holding functions.

4. Are fan motors used in elevator cars?
Fan components may be used in elevator ventilation arrangements depending on the elevator design and application requirements.

5. What is the difference between a door motor and a fan motor?
A door motor is associated with elevator door operation, while a fan motor is associated with ventilation and air circulation functions.
