groups.meta_title: Mitsubishi Elevator Contactor and Filter Components | UAE
groups.meta_description: Find Mitsubishi elevator contactor and filter components in the UAE for electrical control, switching and power system applications.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Contactor and Filter, Mitsubishi Elevator Electrical Components, Elevator Contactor and Filter UAE
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Contactor & Filter

Electrical control systems rely on various components to manage power distribution, switching operations and system performance. Mitsubishi elevator contactor and filter components form part of these electrical arrangements and are available for different elevator applications.

As important Mitsubishi elevator electrical components, contactors are electrically operated switching devices used to control electrical circuits within the elevator system. Different contactor types may be used depending on the operational and electrical requirements of the application.
Filters are associated with selected electrical system applications and may be used to support the performance of power and control arrangements within the elevator system.
The Mitsubishi contactor and filter range includes components designed for use across different elevator control and electrical system configurations.
Available Product Options
Contactors

• SD-N21 (DC125V)
  • SD-N35 (DC125V)
  • SD-N50 (DC125V)
  • SD-N65 (DC125V)
  • SD-N80 (DC125V)
  • SD-N95 (DC125V)
  • SRD-N4
  • SRD-N8
Filter
• LF360CFW7
Product Note: Contactors and filters may vary in ratings, configurations and application requirements depending on the elevator system design.
Functions
Contactors

• Supporting the switching of electrical circuits within elevator systems
  • Assisting the control of electrical power to system components
  • Working as part of elevator control and operational arrangements
Filters

• Supporting selected electrical system applications
  • Working alongside power and control system components
  • Assisting electrical system performance depending on the application
FAQ

1. How do contactors support elevator operation?
Contactors help manage the switching of electrical circuits required for various elevator functions and system operations.

2. Are contactors and filters used in the same part of the elevator system?
Not necessarily. Contactors and filters may be used in different electrical and control system applications depending on the elevator design.

3. Why do elevator systems use multiple electrical components?
Elevator systems rely on different electrical components to perform specific functions such as circuit switching, power management and control system operation.

4. What is the difference between a contactor and a filter?
A contactor is used for electrical switching functions, while a filter is associated with specific electrical system applications involving power or control arrangements.

groups.meta_title: Mitsubishi Elevator Transformer, HOP Box & Display Board | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Mitsubishi elevator transformers, HOP boxes and display board parts for power conversion, passenger information and landing control applications.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator Transformer, Mitsubishi Elevator HOP Box, Mitsubishi Elevator Display Boards
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator Transformer, HOP Box & Display Board

Mitsubishi elevator transformers, display boards and HOP boxes are used in different areas of the elevator system and support electrical, control and passenger communication functions. These components are available in various configurations depending on the elevator model and application requirements.
1. Mitsubishi Elevator Transformers

Mitsubishi elevator transformers are electrical components used to convert incoming building power into the voltage levels required for elevator controls and auxiliary equipment. They form part of the elevator electrical system and support the operation of various control and power-related components.
Available Product Options

• X54DC-02
  • X54GS-18
  • MCE-4
  • X54DH-07
  • YX100C378-02
2. Mitsubishi Elevator Display Boards

Mitsubishi elevator display boards provide visual information to passengers during elevator operation. Often referred to as Car Position Indicators (CPI) or Hall Position Indicators (HPI), they are used to display information such as floor position, travel direction and elevator status.
Available Product Options
Available display board variants include MIDI01 - MIDI19.
Note: MIDI01 - MIDI19 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
3. Mitsubishi Elevator HOP Boxes

Mitsubishi elevator HOP (Hall Operating Panel) boxes are located at each floor landing and contain the components used for passenger call registration and elevator communication functions. They form part of the passenger interface system and support communication between passengers and the elevator control system.
Available Product Options

• GPS-3 Without Display (Simplex)
  • GPS-3 Without Display (Duplex)
Functions

ComponentsFunctions
Elevator TransformerConvert incoming electrical power and support elevator control systems.
Elevator Display BoardDisplay floor position, travel direction and elevator status information.
Elevator HOP boxHouse hall operating panel components and support passenger call functions.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a transformer in a Mitsubishi elevator?
A transformer converts incoming electrical power into the voltage levels required by elevator controls and auxiliary components.

2. What information can be displayed on a Mitsubishi elevator display board?
Display boards may show information such as floor position, travel direction and elevator operating status.

3. What does HOP stand for in an elevator system?
HOP stands for Hall Operating Panel, which is installed at each floor landing and forms part of the passenger call arrangement.

4. What is the difference between a display board and a HOP box?
A display board provides visual elevator information, while a HOP box houses the components used for passenger elevator calls at the landing.

faq.answer: Display boards may show information such as floor position, travel direction and elevator operating status.

groups.alttext_group: MITSUBISHI Elevator Spare Parts PC Boards
groups.meta_title: Buy Mitsubishi Elevator PC Board Parts UAE | Bestech
groups.meta_description: Find Mitsubishi elevator PC boards and electronic control board parts for selected elevator systems and replacement requirements.
groups.keywords: Mitsubishi Elevator PC Boards, Mitsubishi Elevator PCB Boards, Elevator PC Board Parts UAE
paragraph_text: MITSUBISHI Elevator PC Boards

Mitsubishi elevator PC boards (Printed Circuit Boards) are electronic components used within elevator control, communication and monitoring systems. These boards contain the circuitry, processors and electronic components responsible for supporting various elevator functions, including system control, signal processing, communication and operational management.

As the electronic control centre of the elevator system, Mitsubishi elevator PCB boards are used across different applications depending on the elevator model and system requirements. The available range includes multiple PC board series and configurations designed for selected Mitsubishi elevator systems.
Available Mitsubishi Elevator PC Board Types

The Mitsubishi elevator PC board range includes various electronic boards associated with control, communication, monitoring and operating functions within elevator systems. Available categories include:

• Mitsubishi Lift Controller Boards
  • Mitsubishi Communication Boards
  • Mitsubishi Command Boards
  • Mitsubishi Motherboards
  • Mitsubishi Power Supply Boards
  • Mitsubishi Car Top Boards (CTB)
  • Mitsubishi COP Command Boards
  • Mitsubishi Group Control Boards
  • Mitsubishi Hall Indicator Boards
  • Mitsubishi Arrival Gong Boards
  • Mitsubishi UCMP Safety Boards
Product Note: Mitsubishi elevator PC boards are available in different configurations and specifications depending on the elevator model and system requirements.
Functions

Mitsubishi elevator PC boards support various electronic functions within elevator systems, including control, communication, passenger interface, monitoring, indication and safety-related applications. The specific function of each board depends on its design and intended system application.
FAQ

1. What is a Mitsubishi elevator PC board?
A Mitsubishi elevator PC board is an electronic circuit board used within the elevator system to support control, communication and operational functions.

2. What information should be checked before replacing a PC board?
The existing board number, model reference and elevator system details should be verified before selecting a replacement PC board.

3. Why is the board number important when selecting a replacement?
The board number helps identify the correct PC board configuration and compatibility with the existing elevator system.

4. Do PC boards only control elevator movement?
No. In addition to control-related functions, PC boards may also support communication, passenger interface, indication and monitoring applications depending on the board type.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Push Button & Button Plate
groups.meta_title: Buy Otis Elevator Push Buttons & Button Plates | UAE
groups.meta_description: Otis elevator push buttons and button plates available in illuminated designs and multiple finishes for COP and passenger interface applications.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Push Buttons, Otis Button Plates, Elevator Push Button and Button Plate UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Push Button & Button Plate

Otis elevator push buttons form an important part of the passenger interface system used within elevator Car Operating Panels (COP). They are designed to communicate passenger requests to the elevator control system, enabling floor selection and various operational functions.
These buttons may be used for:

• Selecting destination floors
  • Operating door functions
  • Activating door hold or extended open time functions
  • Supporting other elevator operating commands

To suit different elevator applications, Otis elevator push buttons are available in a variety of styles, finishes and illuminated configurations. Paired with compatible button plates, they contribute to the functionality and appearance of elevator operating panels.
1. Otis Elevator Push Buttons

Otis elevator push buttons are installed within elevator operating panels and allow passengers to register floor selections and operational commands. Depending on the application, buttons may include different illumination colours, finishes and configurations.
Available Product Options

• Push Button T-40
  • Button Otis 3200
  • Button BR24
  • Button GAK-AB
  • Button XHB-R34D-A01
  • Button CHVF
Illuminated Otis Push Buttons

Finish TypeAvailable Light Colours (OEM References)
Polished Stainless Steel1.Red (FAA25090A111)
2. Green (FAA25090A112)
3. White (FAA25090A113)
4. Blue (FAA25090A118)
Brass Stainless Steel1. Red (FAA25090A121)
2. Green (FAA25090A122)
3. White (FAA25090A123)
4. Blue (FAA25090A128)
Unpolished Stainless Steel1. Red (FAA25090A311)
2. Green (FAA25090A312)
3. White (FAA25090A313)
4. Blue (FAA25090A318)
2. Otis Button Plates
Otis button plates are associated with elevator operating panel arrangements and provide the mounting surface for compatible push button assemblies.
Available Product Options

• GEN2 Button Plate
  • GEN2 Button Plate SS
Functions
1. Otis Elevator Push Buttons
Support passenger interaction with the elevator system by enabling floor selection and operational command inputs.
2. Otis Button Plates
Provide the mounting arrangement for compatible push button installations within elevator operating panels.
FAQ

1. Why do some elevator push buttons have different illumination colours?
Different illumination colours may be used to improve visibility, indicate button status or match the design requirements of the elevator operating panel.

2. What does the Door Hold button do in an elevator?
The Door Hold or Extended Open Time button is used to keep the elevator doors open for a longer period before they close automatically.

3. Do illuminated push buttons provide visual feedback?
Yes. Illuminated push buttons may provide visual indication that a command has been registered or assist with button visibility during operation.

4. What is the difference between a push button and a button plate?
A push button is the component used for passenger input, while the button plate supports the mounting and installation of the button assembly.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Door & Hanger Roller / Sheave
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Door Hanger Rollers, Guide Wheels & Sheaves | UAE
groups.meta_description: Otis hanger rollers, guide wheels, lock rollers and sheave components are available for elevator door movement and guide rail applications.
groups.keywords: Otis Door Hanger Roller Sheave, Otis Elevator Door Roller Parts, Elevator Door Hanger Roller UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Door & Hanger Roller / Sheave

Otis elevator door rollers, hangers, sheaves and guide assemblies support the movement, guidance and operation of elevator doors, elevator cars and counterweights. These mechanical parts play an important role in door travel, locking arrangements and guide rail movement, helping maintain smooth and reliable elevator operation.

The range includes Otis elevator hanger rollers, door hanger rollers, lock rollers, double slot sheaves, car door hangers, landing door hangers, door knife rollers, roller guide wheels and roller guide shoe assemblies for selected elevator systems.
1. Otis Elevator Hanger Rollers

Otis elevator hanger rollers support the smooth opening and closing of elevator car doors and landing doors. Installed as part of the door support arrangement, they help guide door movement along the overhead track during operation.
Available Product Options

1. 1. Hanger Roller Φ86
  2. 2. AT120 Hanger Roller
    • Φ56 × 16 (White)
    • Φ56 × 16 (Green)
    • Φ56mm
    • Φ65mm
  3. 3. E311 Hanger Roller
2. Otis Door Hanger Rollers

Otis door hanger rollers are high-wear parts that support the weight of elevator door panels and guide their movement along the overhead track. Their operation contributes to smooth door travel, proper alignment and reliable door performance.
Available Product Options

1. 1. Door Hanger Roller Φ140
  2. 2. Door Hanger Roller D2200
  3. 3. Door Hanger Roller Variants:
    • Φ80 × 18, 6204
    • Φ85 × 20, 6204
    • Φ50 × 16, 6200
    • Φ35 × 11 × M12
    • D2200, Φ76 × 20 (OEM: FBA5394A4)
3. Otis Elevator Lock Rollers

Otis elevator lock rollers form part of landing door lock mechanisms and are associated with the operation of door locking arrangements. These rollers assist with the engagement of landing door locks as part of the elevator safety system.
Available Product Options

1. 1. GS09 Lock Roller (Φ37 × 20 × M12)
  2. 2. Lock Roller (Φ37 × 20 × Φ12)
  3. 3. 3200 Door Lock Roller (Φ52 × 20 × Φ12)
4. Otis Door Knife Rollers

Otis door knife rollers form part of the door clutch assembly and help smooth the engagement and release of landing door locks during door operation. They support the interaction between the car door and landing door systems.
Available Product Options
Door Knife Roller Variants
5. Otis Double Slot Sheaves
Otis double slot sheaves are pulley wheels designed with two parallel grooves that guide and support multiple ropes, cables or belts simultaneously within selected elevator applications.
Available Product Options
• Double Slot Sheave Φ140 × 20, 6204
6. Otis Elevator Car Door Hangers

Otis elevator car door hangers are assemblies mounted on top of the elevator car that support and guide car door panels during opening and closing operations. They form part of the door operating arrangement responsible for door movement.
Available Product Options
• Car Door Hanger JJ-700-1100 (TOEC-40 2000VF)
7. Otis Landing Door Hangers

Otis landing door hangers are assemblies installed at each floor landing that support and guide landing door panels during operation. They help maintain door alignment and smooth movement within the hoistway entrance system.
Available Product Options
• Landing Door Hanger JJ-700-1100 (TOEC-40 2000VF)
8. Otis Roller Guide Wheels

Otis roller guide wheels help ensure that elevator cars and counterweights travel smoothly along guide rails. These guide wheels support stable movement and guide rail alignment during elevator operation.
Available Product Options
1. Standard Roller Guide Wheels
2. Hydraulic Roller Guide Wheel
9. Otis Elevator Roller Guide Shoe Assemblies

Otis elevator roller guide shoe assemblies are used to guide elevator cars or counterweights along guide rails. As an alternative to traditional sliding guide arrangements, they support smooth travel and alignment during operation.
Available Product Options

• Φ95 × 26 × Φ16 (Mounting Hole: Φ85 × 50)
  • Φ125 × 22 (6203, Mounting Hole: Φ140 × 76)
  • Φ160 × 30 (6203, Mounting Hole: Φ92 × 80 / Φ140 × 76)
  • Φ76 × 22 (6203, Mounting Hole: Φ92 × 80)
Available OEM References: K-AAA24180AX, KAA24180AAA and KAAAA24180AW.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Hanger RollersSupport and guide elevator door movement along door tracks.
Door Hanger RollersSupport door weight and assist with smooth door travel.
Lock RollersAssociated with landing door locking arrangements and lock engagement.
Door Knife RollersSupport the engagement and release of landing door locks.
Double Slot SheavesGuide and support ropes, cables or belts.
Car Door HangersSupport and guide elevator car door panels.
Landing Door HangersSupport and guide landing door panels.
Roller Guide WheelsGuide elevator cars and counterweights along guide rails.
Roller Guide Shoe AssembliesGuide elevator cars or counterweights along guide rails and support alignment.
FAQ

1. What is the difference between a hanger roller and a door hanger roller?
A hanger roller assists with door movement along the track, while a door hanger roller is specifically designed to support the weight of the door panel and guide its movement.

2. How does a double slot sheave differ from a standard sheave?
A double slot sheave is designed with two grooves, allowing it to guide and support two ropes, cables or belts simultaneously.

3. What is the difference between a lock roller and a door knife roller?
A lock roller is associated with the landing door lock mechanism, while a door knife roller forms part of the door clutch arrangement and assists with the engagement and release of the landing door lock.

4. How do roller guide wheels contribute to elevator operation?
Roller guide wheels help guide the elevator car or counterweight along the guide rails and support smooth travel during operation.

faq.question: What is the difference between a hanger roller and a door hanger roller?
faq.answer: A hanger roller assists with door movement along the track, while a door hanger roller is specifically designed to support the weight of the door panel and guide its movement.

faq.question: How does a double slot sheave differ from a standard sheave?
faq.answer: A double slot sheave is designed with two grooves, allowing it to guide and support two ropes, cables or belts simultaneously.

faq.question: What is the difference between a lock roller and a door knife roller?
faq.answer: A lock roller is associated with the landing door lock mechanism, while a door knife roller forms part of the door clutch arrangement and assists with the engagement and release of the landing door lock.

faq.question: How do roller guide wheels contribute to elevator operation?
faq.answer: Roller guide wheels help guide the elevator car or counterweight along the guide rails and support smooth travel during operation.

groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Guide Shoe Insert Parts | Bestech UAE
groups.meta_description: Find Otis guide shoe inserts, liners and guide shoe components suitable for various elevator guide system requirements.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Guide Shoe Insert, Otis Guide Shoe Inserts, Elevator Guide Shoe Insert UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Guide Shoe & Insert

Otis elevator guide shoe inserts are high-wear parts installed within guide shoe assemblies. They provide the contact surface between the elevator car or counterweight and the steel guide rail, helping support guided movement and alignment during elevator operation.
Often referred to as guide gibs or liners, these inserts form part of the elevator guidance arrangement and are designed to work alongside guide shoes in selected elevator systems.
Available in different dimensions and configurations, Otis guide shoe inserts are used across various elevator guide applications depending on system design and operational requirements.
1. Otis Elevator Guide Shoe Inserts

Otis elevator guide shoe inserts are fitted inside guide shoe assemblies and act as the contact surface between the guide rail and the moving elevator car or counterweight. Available in different dimensions and configurations, these inserts are used across various elevator guide systems.
Available Product Options
Guide Shoe Insert Range

• XIZi Guide Shoe Insert (200 × 25 × 6, White/Black)
  • Standard Dimension Variants
  • Iron Back Variants (150 × 35 × 10mm and 210 × 35 × 10mm)
  • Multi-piece Configurations
  • Import Type and Standard Guide Shoe Inserts
Available in multiple dimensions, widths, thicknesses and configurations for selected elevator guide systems.
2. Otis Elevator Guide Shoes

Otis elevator guide shoes are installed on elevator cars and counterweights and work together with guide shoe inserts to guide movement along the guide rails. These guide arrangements help support alignment and controlled travel during operation.
Available Product Options
1. Guide Shoes for Cabin
• 220 × 10, Mounting Hole: 80 × 65
2. Guide Shoes for Counterweight

• 145 × 10, Mounting Hole: 79 × 50
  • 220 × 16, Mounting Hole: 48 × 82
3. Guide Shoe Variant
• 200 × 40 × 76 × 13
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Guide Shoe InsertsProvide the contact surface between the guide rail and elevator car or counterweight.
Guide ShoesSupport the guidance and alignment of elevator cars and counterweights along guide rails.
FAQ

1. What is the wear surface in a guide shoe assembly?
The guide shoe insert acts as the wear surface between the guide rail and the moving elevator car or counterweight.

2. Why are guide shoe inserts considered high-wear parts?
Guide shoe inserts remain in contact with the guide rail during elevator operation and are therefore subject to continuous friction and wear.

3. Do guide shoes and guide shoe inserts perform the same function?
No. The guide shoe forms part of the guidance arrangement, while the guide shoe insert provides the contact surface that interacts with the guide rail.

4. Where are guide shoe inserts installed in an elevator system?
Guide shoe inserts are installed within guide shoe assemblies fitted to the elevator car, counterweight or both, depending on the elevator system design.

faq.answer: The guide shoe insert acts as the wear surface between the guide rail and the moving elevator car or counterweight.

faq.answer: Guide shoe inserts are installed within guide shoe assemblies fitted to the elevator car, counterweight or both, depending on the elevator system design.

groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Door Sliders and Door Vanes | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis door sliders, door vanes and door vane mechanisms for elevator door guidance, alignment and landing door engagement applications.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Door Sliders and Door Vanes, Otis Door Slider Parts, Elevator Door Slider and Door Vane UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Door Slider & Door Vane

Otis elevator door sliders, door vanes and door vane mechanisms form part of the elevator door operating system and are used for door guidance, alignment and door interaction functions within elevator applications. These products work alongside elevator car and landing door arrangements to help maintain controlled door movement and coordinated door operation.
1. Otis Elevator Door Sliders

Otis elevator door sliders provide lateral stability and guidance to elevator door panels during movement. Installed as part of the door arrangement, they help keep door panels vertical, aligned and properly guided throughout opening and closing operations.
Available Product Options
Door Slider Variants include Door Slider 80/50, Door Slider 70 × 12 and additional variants referenced under OTDS01 - OTDS08.
Note: OTDS01 - OTDS08 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
2. Otis Elevator Door Vanes

Otis elevator door vanes are metal blades or plates mounted as part of the elevator car door arrangement. They are used to mechanically engage and unlock landing doors when the elevator car is levelled at a floor, supporting coordinated door operation between the car and landing entrance.
Available Product Options

• Door Vane (OEM: 10211A)
  • D1 Type Door Vane
  • 3100 Type, AT-120 Door Vane, Left (OEM: FAA24390H1)
  • Door Vane Of Guard Against (OEM: XTA2703AAE)
3. Otis Door Vane Assemblies

Otis door vane assemblies are complete mechanisms mounted on top of the elevator car door and are responsible for engaging and unlocking landing doors during operation. Also referred to as door clutch or retiring cam assemblies, they coordinate the interaction between elevator car doors and landing doors at each floor.
Available Product Options
Available door vane assembly variants include OTDV05, OTDV06 and OTDV07.
Note: These are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Door SlidersSupport door panel guidance, stability and alignment during movement.
Door VanesEngage and unlock landing doors when the elevator car is positioned at a floor.
Door Vane AssembliesCoordinate the engagement and unlocking of landing doors as part of the door operating mechanism.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a door slider in an elevator system?
A door slider helps guide and stabilise elevator door panels during opening and closing operations.

2. How does a door vane interact with landing doors?
A door vane mechanically engages the landing door mechanism when the elevator car is correctly positioned at a floor.

3. What is the difference between a door vane and a door vane assembly?
A door vane is the individual engaging blade or plate, while a door vane assembly is the complete mechanism responsible for engaging and unlocking landing doors.

4. What is a door clutch or retiring cam assembly?
A door clutch or retiring cam assembly is another term used for a door vane assembly that engages and unlocks landing doors during elevator operation.

faq.answer: A door slider helps guide and stabilise elevator door panels during opening and closing operations.

faq.question: How does a door vane interact with landing doors?
faq.answer: A door vane mechanically engages the landing door mechanism when the elevator car is correctly positioned at a floor.

faq.question: What is the difference between a door vane and a door vane assembly?
faq.answer: A door vane is the individual engaging blade or plate, while a door vane assembly is the complete mechanism responsible for engaging and unlocking landing doors.

faq.answer: A door clutch or retiring cam assembly is another term used for a door vane assembly that engages and unlocks landing doors during elevator operation.

groups.meta_title: Otis Door Locks, Contacts & Triangle Keys | Lift Spares

groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Door Lock Contact, Otis Door Lock Contact Parts, Elevator Door Lock Contact UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Door Lock & Contact

Otis elevator door locks and door contacts form part of the safety arrangements used within elevator systems. Door locks are associated with landing door locking arrangements, while door contacts are used for door status verification within the elevator safety circuit. The range also includes triangle locks and triangle keys used for authorised access and emergency door unlocking functions.
These products support selected elevator safety and door operation applications and are available in different designs and configurations.
1. Otis Elevator Door Locks

Otis elevator door locks are used within landing door systems and are associated with door locking and interlock functions. Installed as part of the landing door arrangement, they help maintain door security and controlled access within elevator applications.
Available Product Options
Door Lock Variants

• Door Lock Left GS09
  • Door Lock Left 3100
  • Door Lock, Left (K5)
  • XIZI Door Lock, Left (XAA431R1)
  • Door Lock, 015
  • Landing Door Lock, PB78 (XAA177CB1)
Additional Door Lock Components
• Mandrel For Door Lock
2. Otis Triangle Locks & Keys

Otis triangle locks and triangle keys are associated with emergency access and maintenance functions within elevator door systems. The triangle key is commonly used to operate the emergency unlocking mechanism built into landing doors, allowing authorised personnel to gain access when required.
Available Product Options
• Triangle Lock & Key
3. Otis Elevator Door Contacts

Otis elevator door contacts are safety switches used within the elevator safety circuit. They are designed to verify that elevator car doors or landing doors are fully closed and secured before elevator movement is permitted. These contacts form part of the door monitoring arrangement used within elevator safety systems.
Available Product Options
Door Contact Variants

• Door Contact (MK01-A)
  • Door Contact (TOEC-40-1)
  • Door Contact (GS09-13)
Door Lock Contact Variants

• Door Lock Contact (GS09-1)
  • Additional variants referenced under OTL010 - OTL013
Note: OTL010 - OTL013 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Door LocksSupport landing door locking and interlock applications within elevator systems.
Triangle Locks & KeysProvide access to landing door unlocking mechanisms for maintenance and emergency access purposes.
Door ContactsVerify the closed status of elevator car doors or landing doors within the safety circuit.
FAQ

1. Why do elevator landing doors require a separate locking arrangement?
Landing doors are installed at each floor and require a dedicated locking mechanism as part of the elevator door system and controlled access design.

2. Why are triangle unlocking mechanisms built into landing doors?
Triangle unlocking mechanisms provide authorised access to landing doors for maintenance, inspection and emergency access purposes when required.

3. Why are some door lock products identified by OEM references?
OEM references help identify specific product variants and assist with product selection for compatible elevator applications.

4. Why do elevator landing doors remain closed even when no elevator car is present?
Landing doors are designed to remain closed as part of the door system arrangement at each floor and are only intended to be accessed through the appropriate door mechanisms.

faq.answer: Landing doors are installed at each floor and require a dedicated locking mechanism as part of the elevator door system and controlled access design.

faq.answer: OEM references help identify specific product variants and assist with product selection for compatible elevator applications.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Switches
groups.meta_title: OTIS Elevator Switches & Switch Parts | Bestech UAE
groups.meta_description: Explore OTIS elevator switches and switch parts in the UAE, including mechanical switches, limit switches, COP switches and photoelectric switches.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Switches, Otis Elevator Switch Parts, Elevator Switches UAE, Otis Lift Switches Parts
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Switches

OTIS elevator switches are essential components that help monitor, control and protect various elevator functions. Designed for dependable performance, these switches support operational commands, position monitoring and safety mechanisms throughout the lift system. Whether used within control panels, door systems or safety circuits, OTIS elevator switch parts contribute to the smooth and reliable operation of elevators.

At Bestech, we supply a range of OTIS lift switches parts suitable for maintenance, repair and replacement requirements. Our collection includes mechanical switches, limit switches, COP switches and photoelectric switches used across selected OTIS elevator applications. Businesses seeking quality OTIS Elevator Switches can rely on our product range for compatible and efficient solutions.
Product Range
1. Mechanical Switches

A mechanical switch is a physical device that uses moving components to make or break an electrical circuit in response to movement, position or pressure. These switches are commonly used to detect operational conditions and support elevator control functions.
2. Limit Switches

Limit switches are important safety devices designed to prevent the elevator car and counterweight from travelling beyond their intended operating limits, enhancing overall system safety. They help protect the elevator car and counterweight from overtravel, enhancing overall system safety.
3. COP Switches

COP (Car Operating Panel) switches refer to the control switches located inside the elevator car. They enable passengers to interact with the elevator by activating specific functions through the operating panel.
4. Photoelectric Switches

Photoelectric switches use light-based sensing technology to detect the presence, position or movement of objects without direct physical contact. These switches are used in selected elevator applications requiring non-contact detection.
Available OEM Options

• Mechanical Switches: A6098B11, A6098B15, A6098B3, A6098B7, J06098E5 (6088), G098D1 and G098D3.
  • Limit Switches: AA1, GAA177HA1/112, GAA177-121, GAA177FD/122B and GAA177HA/GAA177-111.
  • Limit Switches of Speed Governor: AA3, TAA177AH1 and TAA177AH2.
  • 1370 Limit Switch: XAA177BW1.
  • XIZI COP Switches: SDK3-3 and CA40-2501/3.
  • Photoelectric Switches: E3S-GS3B4.
Why Choose Bestech?

• Wide range of OTIS elevator switches for various applications.
  • Quality replacement components sourced to meet industry requirements.
  • Suitable solutions for maintenance and repair projects.
  • OEM reference options for easier product identification.
  • Reliable supply of elevator switches in the UAE.
For enquiries about OTIS Elevator Switches and OTIS lift switches parts, contact our team to find the right replacement components for your elevator requirements.
FAQ

1. Why is a limit switch important in an elevator?
Limit switches help prevent the elevator from travelling beyond its designated limits, improving safety.

2. What should I do if I cannot find the switch I need?
Contact our team with the OEM number or product details, and we'll help you identify the suitable replacement part.

3. When should an elevator switch be replaced?
Elevator switches should be replaced if they show signs of wear, damage or inconsistent performance.

4. What information should I provide when enquiring about a switch?
Sharing the OEM number, switch type or elevator model can help us recommend the correct part more quickly.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Sensors
groups.meta_title: OTIS Elevator Sensors & Sensor Parts | Bestech UAE
groups.meta_description: Shop OTIS elevator sensor parts in the UAE, including photo sensors, levelling sensors and speed sensors that support safety, accurate levelling and smooth elevator performance.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Sensors, Otis Elevator Sensor Parts, Elevator Sensors UAE, Otis Lift Sensors Parts
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Sensors

OTIS elevator sensors are designed to detect, monitor and communicate critical information that keeps elevator systems operating accurately and safely. From protecting passengers at the doorway to ensuring precise floor alignment and speed monitoring, these components play an important role in delivering a smooth and reliable elevator experience.

At Bestech, we offer a range of OTIS Elevator Sensor Parts for maintenance, servicing and replacement requirements. Our collection includes photo sensors, levelling sensors and speed sensors used across selected OTIS elevator applications.
Product Range
1. Photo Sensors
Photo sensors act as a protective barrier at the elevator entrance. They detect passengers or objects in the doorway and help prevent the doors from closing unexpectedly, improving passenger safety.
2. Levelling Sensors
Levelling sensors ensure the elevator car stops accurately in line with the landing floor. This precise positioning supports comfortable entry and exit while enhancing accessibility.
3. Speed Sensors
Speed sensors continuously monitor the elevator's operating speed and provide data to the control system. This helps maintain ride comfort, accurate levelling and safe elevator performance.
Available Sensor Models
Photo Sensors: Available in OEM options including SSGD-1L (NC) and SSGD-1H (NO).
Proximity Sensors: Magnetic Proximity Sensor (OEM: MPS-1600-OTIS).
Levelling Sensors: GLS126 Levelling Sensor (NO/NC), SGD-ADS-1, SGD-ADS-2, SGD-100A, SH-ADS-B, SGD-3EF and SSGD-2LM(S).
Speed Sensors: Available in OEM option SGD-PK126RS.
FAQ

1. Why do elevators use multiple sensors?
Different sensors perform different functions, such as door protection, floor levelling and speed monitoring, to help ensure safe and efficient operation.

2. How do photo sensors improve passenger safety?
They detect passengers or objects near the elevator entrance and help prevent the doors from closing unexpectedly.

3. What role does a speed sensor play in an elevator?
Speed sensors continuously monitor the elevator's operating speed to support ride comfort, accurate stopping and safe performance.

4. Do elevator sensors affect ride quality?
Yes. Sensors provide real-time information that helps the elevator move smoothly, stop accurately and operate reliably.

5. Are elevator sensors important for accessibility?
Yes. Sensors such as levelling sensors help ensure the elevator stops accurately at floor level, making access easier for wheelchair users and other passengers.

groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Oil Boxes & Felts | Lift Spares UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis oil boxes and oil box felts for guide rail lubrication and elevator maintenance applications.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Oil Box Felt, Otis Oil Box Felt Parts, Elevator Oil Box Felt UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Oil Box & Felt

Otis elevator oil boxes, also known as oil cups or guide rail lubricators, are designed to store and distribute lubricant within elevator guide rail systems. Working alongside oil box felts, they help transfer lubricant to the guide rail as the elevator car and counterweight travel. These products support guide rail lubrication arrangements in selected elevator systems.
1. Otis Elevator Oil Boxes

Otis elevator oil boxes are offered in a variety of designs and configurations for guide rail lubrication applications. The range includes square, round and OEM-referenced variants suitable for selected elevator car and counterweight systems.
Available Product Options
Oil Box Variants

• Oil Box Square (OTOC01, OTOC03)
  • Oil Box Round (OTOC02)
  • Oil Box (OEM: XAA349E1/E2)
Note: OTOC01, OTOC02 and OTOC03 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
2. Otis Elevator Oil Box Felts

Otis elevator oil box felts are installed within oil box arrangements and help retain and transfer lubricant to guide rail surfaces. Available in different sizes, they are suitable for selected guide rail lubrication systems.
Available Product Options
Oil Box Felt Variants

• Oil Box Felt, 40 × 36 × 36
  • Oil Box Felt, 51 × 38 × 13
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Oil BoxesHold lubricant and support its controlled distribution along elevator guide rail systems.
Oil Box FeltsRetain lubricant and facilitate its transfer from the oil box to the guide rail during operation.
FAQ

1. Why are oil box felts used within elevator lubrication systems?
Oil box felts are commonly used within oil box arrangements to retain and transfer lubricant as part of the guide rail lubrication process.

2. Why do guide rail lubrication systems use both an oil box and a felt?
Guide rail lubrication systems use both an oil box and a felt because each performs a different function. The oil box stores lubricant, while the felt helps retain and transfer lubricant to the guide rail.

3. Can oil box felts be replaced without replacing the oil box?
Oil box felts and oil boxes are supplied as separate products and may be selected or replaced according to application and maintenance requirements.

4. Why do guide rail lubrication systems use both an oil box and a felt?
Oil boxes and oil box felts perform different functions within the lubrication arrangement. The oil box acts as the lubricant reservoir, while the felt helps retain and transfer lubricant to the guide rail.

paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Inspection Box, Control & Intercom
Otis elevator inspection boxes, intercom systems and control power components support elevator monitoring, communication and control functions within selected elevator systems.

The range includes car top inspection boxes, pit inspection boxes, intercom units, power supply components and related control devices designed for maintenance, communication and operational applications.
1. Otis Elevator Inspection Boxes
Otis elevator inspection boxes are control units installed in locations such as the elevator car top or pit area and are associated with inspection, maintenance and operational control functions.
Available in different configurations, these products support selected elevator service and monitoring purposes.
Available Product Options
Inspection Box Variants

• XiZi Car Top Inspection Box (OEM: YK-1B)
  • XiZi Pit Inspection Box (OEM: YK-1A-10)
  • Car Top Inspection Box (OEM: DAA24831F2)
Note: Additional Car Top Inspection Box variants are available under internal product reference codes (e.g., OTIB series) used for identification and product selection.
2. Otis Elevator Intercom Systems

Otis elevator intercom systems provide communication between the elevator car and designated monitoring, security or service locations. These products are associated with elevator communication arrangements and are available in different models for selected applications.
Available Product Options
Intercom Variants

• Intercom, 601WF-B6 (OEM: XAA25302B6)
  • Intercom, 4005WF-E3 (OEM: DAA25301E3)
3. Otis Elevator Control Power & Control Devices

Otis elevator control power components support the supply of electrical power to selected elevator control and communication systems. The range also includes rope brake control devices and related power components for selected elevator functions.
Available Product Options
Control Power Supply Variants

• The Power For Intercom Model V (OEM: DAA25301E5)
  • The Power For Intercom Model V (OEM: DAA25301E10)
  • Control Power Supply (OEM: DAA21190A4)
Control Device
• Rope Brake Control Device (OEM: JSQ-12-C)
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Inspection BoxesSupport elevator inspection, maintenance and operational control functions.
Intercom SystemsProvide two-way communication between the elevator car and designated monitoring or service locations.
Control Power SuppliesSupply power to selected elevator control, communication and electronic system components.
FAQ

1. Why are inspection controls positioned on top of the elevator car?
Inspection controls are positioned to provide convenient access to selected elevator control functions during inspection, testing and maintenance activities.

2. Who typically receives communications from an elevator intercom system?
Communications may be directed to designated monitoring locations such as security desks, building management offices or service providers, depending on the system arrangement.

3. Why do elevator communication systems require a dedicated power supply?
Communication systems may require dedicated power components to support their operation independently within elevator applications.

4. Why are inspection boxes installed in different locations such as the car top and pit area?
Different inspection box locations provide access to selected inspection and maintenance functions associated with different areas of the elevator system.

groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Electric Bell Parts | Lift Spares UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator electric bells and bell components for alarm signalling, passenger notification and elevator maintenance requirements.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Electric Bell, Otis Elevator Bell Parts, Elevator Electric Bell UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Electric Bell

Otis elevator electric bells are alarm signalling devices used within elevator systems. They generate an audible alarm signal when the passenger emergency alarm button is activated inside the elevator car. Installed as part of the elevator alarm arrangement, these products are associated with passenger notification and alarm signalling functions.
The range includes electric bells and related bell components available for selected elevator maintenance and replacement requirements.
Available Product Options
Electric Bell Variants

• Electric Bell (OTIB12)
  • Board For Electric Bell (OEM: GCA23550B1, Internal Code: OTIB11)
Note: OTIB11 and OTIB12 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
Functions

• Provide an audible alarm signal within elevator systems.
  • Operate as part of the elevator alarm arrangement.
  • Support emergency alarm signalling functions.
FAQ

1. What happens when the emergency alarm button is pressed inside an elevator?
When the emergency alarm button is activated, the elevator electric bell generates an audible alarm signal as part of the elevator alarm arrangement.

2. Why do elevators use an audible alarm device?
Audible alarm devices provide a clear alarm signal that can be heard by nearby personnel when the elevator alarm function is activated.

3. Do electric bells operate continuously during normal elevator use?
Electric bells are not intended to operate continuously during normal elevator use. They are designed to generate an audible signal when the alarm function is activated.

4. Can elevator alarm signalling components be replaced individually?
Depending on the product arrangement, electric bells and related bell components may be available as separate replacement parts.

paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Steel Cable, Strip & Belt

Otis elevator steel cables, steel strips, door belts, belt monitoring devices and traction machines are associated with elevator movement, suspension, door operation and monitoring functions. The range includes cable, belt and related components used in selected traction elevator and door operating systems.
1. Otis Elevator Steel Cables
Otis elevator steel cables are used within traction elevator systems to suspend and support the movement of elevator cars and counterweights.
Available Product Options
Steel Cable Variants

• VV30 × 10 Cores (OEM: AAA717AJ2)
  • VV30 × 12 Cores (OEM: AAA717W1)
  • VV60 × 24 Cores (OEM: AAA717R1)
  • VV30 × 12 Cores (OEM: AAA717X1)
  • VV30 × 10 Cores (OEM: AAA717AM2)
  • VV60 × 24 Cores (OEM: AAA717AD1)
2. Otis Elevator Steel Strips & Sliders
Otis elevator steel strips are flat belt components associated with elevator suspension and movement arrangements in selected elevator systems.
Available Product Options

• TOEC-40 Steel Strip
  • E44 Steel Strip
  • Steel Strip Slider, 92 × 37 × 8.5
3. Otis Elevator Door Belts
Otis elevator door belts are associated with door operating arrangements and are used to transmit movement within selected elevator door systems.
Available Product Options
• Door Belt DO2000
4. Otis Traction Belt Detection Devices

Otis traction belt detection devices are monitoring components associated with traction belt arrangements used in selected elevator systems. These products are available in different configurations for belt monitoring and maintenance-related applications.
Available Product Options

• 3 Belt Traction Belt Detection Device (OEM: FAA21700F1 / ABA21700X1)
  • 5 Belt Traction Belt Detection Device (OEM: FAA21700F2 / ABA21700X2)
5. Otis Elevator Traction Machines

Otis elevator traction machines are electro-mechanical units that provide the power and motion required to raise and lower elevator cars and counterweights within traction elevator systems. Often described as the driving unit of the elevator system, they are associated with elevator movement and control functions.
Functions

• Steel Cables: Suspend and support the movement of elevator cars and counterweights within traction elevator systems.
  • Steel Strips: Provide suspension and movement functions within belt-based elevator systems.
  • Steel Strip Sliders: Support the operation and guidance of steel strip arrangements within selected elevator systems.
  • Door Belts: Transmit movement from the door operator to the elevator door panels during opening and closing operations.
  • Traction Belt Detection Devices: Monitor traction belt conditions within selected elevator systems.
  • Traction Machines: Provide the power and motion required to move elevator cars and counterweights within traction elevator systems.
FAQ

1. Why do some elevator systems use steel strips instead of traditional cables?
Steel strips and steel cables are different suspension technologies used within selected elevator systems and applications.

2. Why are traction belt detection devices used in elevator systems?
Traction belt detection devices are associated with the monitoring of traction belt arrangements within selected elevator systems.

3. Are steel cables and door belts used for the same purpose?
No. Steel cables are associated with elevator suspension and movement functions, while door belts are associated with door operating arrangements.

4. What is the difference between a traction machine and a traction belt detection device?
A traction machine provides the power required for elevator movement, while a traction belt detection device is associated with monitoring traction belt arrangements.

paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Service Tool

Otis elevator service tools are specialised diagnostic and communication devices used by trained technicians to interact with elevator control systems. Commonly referred to as service tool interfaces or maintenance tools, these products are used to establish communication with the elevator controller during inspection, maintenance and troubleshooting activities.

These tools are associated with system monitoring, diagnostic and controller communication functions within selected elevator applications. The range includes service tool variants available for elevator diagnostic and maintenance requirements.
Available Product Options

• Service Tool (OEM: GAA21750S1)
  • Service Tool (Work Worldwide) (OEM: GAA21750AK3)
Product Highlights

• Used by trained technicians during elevator maintenance and service procedures.
  • Provides a communication interface with elevator control systems.
  • Associated with diagnostic, monitoring and troubleshooting activities.
  • Available in multiple variants for selected elevator applications.
FAQ

1. What type of information can be accessed through an elevator service tool?
An elevator service tool can be used to access selected controller and system-related information through direct communication with the elevator control system.

2. Why is direct communication with the elevator controller important?
Direct communication allows technicians to access system information and carry out diagnostic and service procedures more efficiently.

3. Are service tools used only when an elevator develops a fault?
No. Service tools may be used during routine inspection, monitoring, maintenance and diagnostic activities.

4. Can elevator operation be monitored through a service tool?
Yes. Service tools are associated with system monitoring functions and may be used during inspection and maintenance activities to access selected operational and controller-related information.

groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Encoder Components | Lift Spares UAE
groups.meta_description: Otis elevator encoder components designed for speed monitoring and position measurement functions within elevator systems.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Encoder, Otis Encoder Parts, Elevator Encoder UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Encoder

Otis elevator encoders are precision digital sensors that measure the speed and position of the elevator car during operation. The information generated by the encoder is used by the elevator control system to monitor movement and support smooth elevator performance.
Available in different models and specifications, Otis encoders form part of selected traction elevator systems and are associated with elevator control, monitoring and positioning functions.
Available Product Options

Encoder ModelOEM Reference
SBH2-1024-2T (30-050-16)DAAG33D1
ZKT-D100H30B-102.4B-R7.5-15FDAAG33D2

IG06-0713/ABX-500-PPABX-500-PP
OH48-3600C/T-L3-5VAAAG33AG22
0IH76-4096C/T-L2C2-15VAAAG33AJ1
Additional encoder variants are available under OEM references AAAG3324, AAAG3326, DO2000-5T, DO2000-7T and GCA633A1.
Functions

• Measure elevator car speed within elevator control systems.
  • Monitor elevator car position during operation.
  • Supply speed and position data to elevator control systems.
  • Assist with elevator movement monitoring and control processes.
FAQ

1. Can an encoder measure both speed and position?
Yes. Elevator encoders are associated with both speed measurement and position monitoring functions within selected elevator systems.

2. Why is speed measurement important in an elevator system?
Speed measurement provides information that helps the elevator control system monitor and manage elevator movement during operation.

3. Why does an elevator controller require position information?
Position information helps the elevator control system monitor the location of the elevator car during operation.

4. Why are different encoder models available for elevator systems?
Different elevator systems may require encoder models with specific specifications and configurations depending on the application.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Door Motor,Door Power & Control Box
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Door Motors & Control Boxes | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator door motors, door control boxes and door power components for door movement and control requirements.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Door Motor, Otis Door Power Control Box, Elevator Door Motor and Control Box UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Door Motor, Door Power & Control Box

The opening and closing of elevator doors rely on the coordinated operation of components responsible for movement, control and power functions. Otis elevator door motors, door control boxes and door power units form part of this arrangement and are associated with the operation of elevator door systems.
1. Otis Elevator Door Motors

Otis elevator door motors provide the driving force required for the controlled movement of elevator door panels. Installed as part of the door operator arrangement, these motors are associated with door opening and closing functions within selected elevator systems.
Available door motor variants include AT120, DO2000 and XRDS-80J1 80W DC110V/1.1A with OEM Number XAA20500H1.
2. Otis Elevator Door Control Boxes

Otis elevator door control boxes are electronic control units associated with the management of elevator door operation. These components are used within door operator arrangements and are associated with door control, monitoring and operational functions.

Available door control box variants include DCSS, DCSS IV, DCSS V E and AT120 door control box variants, together with additional models identified by OEM references GBA24350BBH1, GBA24350AW11, GHA24350BD11, GFA24350AW11, FAA24350BK1 and GJA24350BD11.
3. Otis Elevator Door Power Units

Otis elevator door power units are associated with the power requirements of selected elevator door operating systems. These components are available for use within door control and door operator arrangements.
Available door power variant is AT120 with OEM Number FAA24350BM3.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Door MotorsProvide the driving force required for the controlled opening and closing of elevator door panels.
Door Control BoxesManage and coordinate elevator door operating, control and monitoring functions.
Door Power UnitsSupply power for selected elevator door operating arrangements.
FAQ

1. Why do elevator door systems use multiple components instead of a single unit?
Elevator door systems use separate components for movement, control and power functions, allowing each part to perform a specific role within the door operating arrangement.

2. How is door movement generated within an elevator system?
Door movement is generated by the door motor as part of the elevator door operating arrangement.

3. What is meant by a door operating arrangement?
A door operating arrangement refers to the group of components responsible for the movement, control and operation of elevator doors.

4. What happens behind the scenes when an elevator door opens?
The opening of an elevator door involves the coordinated operation of components responsible for door movement, control and power functions.

faq.answer: Elevator door systems use separate components for movement, control and power functions, allowing each part to perform a specific role within the door operating arrangement.

faq.answer: Door movement is generated by the door motor as part of the elevator door operating arrangement.

faq.question: What is meant by a door operating arrangement?
faq.answer: A door operating arrangement refers to the group of components responsible for the movement, control and operation of elevator doors.

faq.answer: The opening of an elevator door involves the coordinated operation of components responsible for door movement, control and power functions.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Brake, Machine& Fan
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Brake, Machine & Fan Parts | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator brake components, traction machines and fan parts for braking, elevator movement and ventilation functions.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Brake and Fan Parts, Otis Elevator Machine Brake, Elevator Brake and Fan Components UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Brake, Machine & Fan
Elevator performance depends on components responsible for stopping, holding, driving and ventilating the system.
Otis brake components, traction machines and fans contribute to these functions through machine braking arrangements, elevator movement systems and passenger ventilation arrangements.
1. Otis Elevator Brake Components

Otis elevator brake components form part of the main machine brake assembly and are associated with stopping and holding functions within traction elevator systems. The range includes brake coils, brake drums, brake bars and brake pads used in selected machine brake arrangements.
Available Product Options

Brake ComponentAvailable Variants
Brake CoilsBrake Coil (OEM: TAB330AJ1), 13VTR-150mm (OEM: DAA230E2), 18ATF, DZE-14 (OEM: DAA330K5), 17CT
Brake Drums13VTR, 17CT
Brake Bars17CT
Brake Pads13VTR (OTBM18, OTBM22), W1000, 19BT, 17CT, 18ATF
Note: OTBM18 and OTBM22 are internal product reference codes used for identification and product selection purposes.
2. Otis Elevator Traction Machines

Otis elevator traction machines are electro-mechanical units that provide the power and motion required to raise and lower elevator cars and counterweights within traction elevator systems. Often described as the driving unit of the elevator system, they are associated with elevator movement and control functions.
Traction Machine Variants include 13VTR, 16VEC, 17CT and 18ATF.
3. Otis Elevator Fans

Otis elevator fans are ventilation components designed to circulate air within the elevator car. Installed to support passenger comfort, these products assist with air movement inside selected elevator cabins.
Available product options include Fan for 17CT Machine.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Brake CoilsControl the electromagnet function within the main machine brake assembly.
Brake DrumsForm part of the brake assembly involved in stopping and holding functions.
Brake BarsSupport brake operation within selected machine brake arrangements.
Brake PadsProvide the friction surface required for stopping and holding the elevator car and counterweight.
Traction MachinesProvide the power and motion required to move elevator cars and counterweights.
FansCirculate air within the elevator car to support passenger comfort.
FAQ

1. Why are ventilation fans installed inside elevator cars?
Ventilation fans circulate air within the elevator cabin to support passenger comfort during travel.

2. What keeps an elevator from moving when it is stopped?
The machine brake assembly is responsible for stopping and holding the elevator car and counterweight when required.

3. Why do elevators still need a brake if they already have a traction machine?
The traction machine provides movement, while the brake assembly is responsible for stopping and holding the elevator system.

4. What happens when braking force is released during elevator operation?
When braking force is released, the traction machine provides the motion required for elevator travel within the system.

faq.answer: Ventilation fans circulate air within the elevator cabin to support passenger comfort during travel.

faq.question: What keeps an elevator from moving when it is stopped?
faq.answer: The machine brake assembly is responsible for stopping and holding the elevator car and counterweight when required.

faq.question: What happens when braking force is released during elevator operation?
faq.answer: When braking force is released, the traction machine provides the motion required for elevator travel within the system.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Oil Seal & Sealing Cover
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Oil Seal & Sealing Cover Parts | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator oil seals and sealing covers designed to help contain lubricants and protect critical elevator components.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Oil Seal and Sealing Cover, Otis Elevator Sealing Components, Elevator Oil Seal Parts UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Oil Seal & Sealing Cover

Keeping fluids contained and sensitive components protected is essential for the reliable operation of elevator systems. Otis oil seals and sealing covers contribute to these requirements by helping prevent leakage and shielding selected mechanisms from dust, moisture and debris.
1. Otis Elevator Oil Seals
Otis elevator oil seals are designed to help prevent the leakage of lubricating oil or hydraulic fluid while accommodating the movement of rotating or reciprocating components.
Their integrity contributes to the cleanliness and proper functioning of selected elevator mechanisms.
Available Product Options

• Oil Seal For 13VTR
  • Oil Seal For 17CT, Small
  • Oil Seal For 17CT, Big
  • Oil Seal For 19BT
2. Otis Elevator Sealing Covers

Otis elevator sealing covers are designed to shield selected components from dust, moisture and debris while helping contain lubricants where required. These products contribute to maintaining the cleanliness and proper function of the mechanisms they protect.
Available product options include Sealing Cover for 13VTR.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Oil SealsPrevent the leakage of lubricating oil or hydraulic fluid. Accommodate the movement of rotating or reciprocating components. Maintain sealing performance during component movement.
Sealing CoversHelp protect components from dust, moisture and debris. Assist in containing lubricants where required. Shield sensitive mechanisms from external contaminants.
FAQ

1. Why is preventing fluid leakage important in elevator systems?
Preventing fluid leakage helps maintain cleanliness and supports the proper functioning of selected elevator mechanisms.

2. Can a component be protected even if it is not exposed to passengers?
Yes. Internal elevator components may require protection from dust, moisture and debris to help maintain their operation.

3. Why do some elevator components require both sealing and protection?
Some components need to contain lubricants while also being protected from external contaminants such as dust and moisture.

4. What types of movement can an oil seal accommodate?
Oil seals are designed to function with components that rotate or move in a reciprocating manner.

faq.answer: Preventing fluid leakage helps maintain cleanliness and supports the proper functioning of selected elevator mechanisms.

faq.question: Can a component be protected even if it is not exposed to passengers?
faq.answer: Yes. Internal elevator components may require protection from dust, moisture and debris to help maintain their operation.

faq.answer: Some components need to contain lubricants while also being protected from external contaminants such as dust and moisture.

faq.question: What types of movement can an oil seal accommodate?
faq.answer: Oil seals are designed to function with components that rotate or move in a reciprocating manner.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Frequency Inverter
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Frequency Inverter Drives | BESTECH UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator frequency inverters and inverter drives designed to control elevator motor speed, direction and torque.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Frequency Inverter, Otis Elevator Inverter Drive, Elevator Frequency Inverter UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Frequency Inverter

Otis elevator frequency inverters, commonly referred to as VFD or VVVF drives, are electronic control devices used to regulate the operation of elevator motors. By controlling speed, direction and torque, they contribute to smooth elevator movement and controlled motor performance within selected elevator systems.
Available Product Options
OVF30 Series

• OOVF30
  • OVF30/70A (OEM: ACA21290AK2)
  • OVF30/90A (OEM: ACA21290BJ2)
  • OVF30/120A (OEM: ACA221290BA2)
OVF20 Series

• OVF20
  • OVF20/9KW (OEM: GBA21343J100)
  • OVF20/15KW (OEM: GCA21150D1)
Functions

• Control the speed of elevator motor operation.
  • Regulate motor direction during elevator travel.
  • Manage motor torque within selected elevator systems.
  • Support precise control of elevator motor behaviour.
FAQ

1. Why doesn't an elevator motor run at a fixed speed?
Elevator systems require controlled changes in motor operation during travel, which is managed through the frequency inverter.

2. What does torque control mean in an elevator system?
Torque control refers to the regulation of the motor's turning force during elevator operation.

3. Can a frequency inverter influence the direction of elevator travel?
Yes. Frequency inverters are used to regulate motor direction as part of elevator operation.

4. Can a frequency inverter control more than just motor speed?
Yes. Frequency inverters are associated with the control of motor speed, direction and torque within elevator systems.

faq.question: Why doesn't an elevator motor run at a fixed speed?
faq.answer: Elevator systems require controlled changes in motor operation during travel, which is managed through the frequency inverter.

faq.question: What does torque control mean in an elevator system?
faq.answer: Torque control refers to the regulation of the motor's turning force during elevator operation.

faq.question: Can a frequency inverter influence the direction of elevator travel?
faq.answer: Yes. Frequency inverters are used to regulate motor direction as part of elevator operation.

faq.question: Can a frequency inverter control more than just motor speed?
faq.answer: Yes. Frequency inverters are associated with the control of motor speed, direction and torque within elevator systems.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts Display Board & Outbound Board
groups.meta_title: Otis Elevator Display Board & Outbound Board Parts | UAE
groups.meta_description: Buy Otis elevator display boards and outbound boards designed to communicate elevator position and operational status information.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator Display Board, Otis Elevator Outbound Board, Elevator Display Board Parts UAE
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator Display Board & Outbound Board

Visual indicators play an important role in communicating elevator status to passengers. Otis display boards provide information such as the elevator's current position and operating status, while outbound boards form part of selected display arrangements responsible for presenting status information.
1. Otis Car Display Boards

Otis elevator car display boards, commonly referred to as car position indicators, are the primary visual communication devices used inside the elevator car. Positioned above the door or integrated into the Car Operating Panel (COP), they help passengers stay informed about the elevator's current status and location during travel.
2. Otis Elevator Display Boards

Otis elevator display boards serve as visual interfaces that present operational updates received from the elevator system. Depending on the display arrangement, they communicate information relating to elevator position and operational status through visual indicators.
3. Otis Elevator Outbound Boards
Otis outbound boards form part of selected visual indication arrangements within elevator systems and contribute to the presentation of elevator status information.
Available Product Options

Product GroupVariants
Car Display BoardsCar Display Board (OEM: XAA25140AP2), Car Interior Display Board (OEM: XAA25140AB2)
Outbound BoardsOutbound Board (OEM: DAA2680062)
DAA25140 SeriesDAA25140NNNS, DAA25140NNNG, DAA25140NNN13, DAA25140NNN14, DAA25140NNP2, DAA25140NNP14
JAA25140 SeriesJAA25140AAF102, JAA25140AAD122, JAA25140AAD126, JAA25140AAF126
DAA25250 SeriesDAA25250A202, DAA25250A203, DAA25250A204
Additional Display Board ModelsCXAA25140AH13, FAA25100F, FAA25100AA2, FAA25100K1, DAA26800AM, DAA26800AS, STN430-V3.2, XBA23550B1, XBA23550B2, XAA23550B3, XBA23550B4, CHVF, TOEC-40, FDA23600V1, ACA26800AJX1, ACA26800HJ1, FBA23600V1, DCE2350167
Note: Display Board, New (OEM: DAA25140NNNG) is also identified under internal product reference codes OTD105 and OTD106 used for product identification and selection purposes.
Note: FDA23600V1 and FBA23600V1 are available in display board and include-box configurations.
Functions
Display Boards

• Show the elevator's current floor or position during operation.
  • Indicate operational information through visual displays.
  • Provide visual communication as part of the elevator indication arrangement.
Outbound Boards

• Support visual indication arrangements associated with elevator operation.
  • Present status-related information within selected elevator systems.
  • Assist in extending visual indication functions beyond the elevator car.
FAQ

1. Why do some elevator systems use multiple display boards instead of a single display?
Different display boards may be installed in different locations to provide elevator information where it is needed.

2. Why do display boards have different OEM references even though they perform similar functions?
Display boards are available in different designs and configurations to match selected elevator models and control arrangements.

3. Do elevator display boards only show floor numbers?
No. Depending on the display arrangement, they may also indicate the elevator's operating status.

4. Why do some display boards include a box?
The box forms part of the display arrangement and provides housing for the display assembly.

faq.answer: No. Depending on the display arrangement, they may also indicate the elevator's operating status.

faq.answer: The box forms part of the display arrangement and provides housing for the display assembly.

groups.alttext_group: OTIS Elevator Spare Parts PC Board (ALL MAIN, INVERTER, CONTROL, COMMUNICATION)
groups.meta_title: Buy Otis Elevator PC Boards & PCB Control Boards | UAE
groups.meta_description: Explore Otis elevator PC boards for main control, inverter, communication and door operation requirements.
groups.keywords: Otis Elevator PC Boards, Otis Elevator PCB Boards, Otis Elevator Control Boards
paragraph_text: OTIS Elevator PC Board

Otis elevator PC boards are electronic control components that manage, monitor and coordinate various elevator functions. These boards process signals, exchange information and support systems related to elevator movement, door operation, communication and safety functions.
The range includes main control boards, inverter boards, communication boards and other specialised PC boards used within selected elevator applications.
1. Main PC Boards

Otis main PC boards act as the central control units responsible for managing elevator operations, safety functions and system communication. Often regarded as the "brain" of the elevator, these boards coordinate the activities of multiple elevator subsystems.
2. Inverter & Drive PC Boards

Otis inverter and drive PC boards manage the processing and control functions associated with the Variable Frequency Drive (VFD). These boards contribute to the regulation of elevator motor performance and inverter operation.
3. Communication PC Boards

Otis communication PC boards facilitate the exchange of information between the elevator controller and peripheral devices throughout the elevator system. They support reliable data transmission and communication functions.
4. Door Machine PC Boards
Otis door machine PC boards are dedicated electronic boards associated with elevator door operation. These boards manage door-related control functions within the door operator arrangement.
5. Brake Control PC Boards
Otis brake control PC boards are associated with the electronic control of elevator braking functions and contribute to the operation of selected brake arrangements.
6. Additional PC Board Categories
The range also includes weighting boards, parallel boards, power supply boards, specialised control boards and other PC board variants associated with specific elevator functions.
Available Product Options

Product CategoryExample Variants
Main PC BoardsA9693D2, GEA21270A1, GCA26800KF1
Inverter & Drive PC BoardsABA26800GW4, GDA26800J1, ABA26800XU1
Communication PC BoardsAAA26800GG1, GBA25005C2, DAA25005C1
Door Machine PC BoardsJAA26805AAA001, B9693MB1
Brake Control PC BoardsGAA26800LB1, GBA26800LB2
Weighting PC BoardsGBA26800KJ1, FAA25402AF1
Parallel PC BoardsSOM-A9693F1, GAA26800AL1
Power Supply PC BoardsGCA26800J5, GAA26800KP1
Control PC BoardsAMCB2, ADA26800XB1
GEN-II PC BoardsGAA26810C1, GAA26810C2
Additional PC Board VariantsKBA26800AAB, RS14-White, FAA2500E2
Note: The examples listed above represent selected variants within each category. Additional OEM-referenced PC board models are also available.
Functions

Product CategoryFunctions
Main PC BoardsCoordinate elevator operations, safety functions and system communication.
Inverter & Drive PC BoardsManage processing and control functions associated with inverter operation.
Communication PC BoardsFacilitate data exchange between controllers and peripheral devices.
Door Machine PC BoardsManage door-related control functions within door operator arrangements.
Brake Control PC BoardsSupport the electronic management of selected elevator braking arrangements.
Weighting PC BoardsAssist with functions associated with elevator load and weighing arrangements.
Parallel PC BoardsSupport signal transmission and parallel communication functions.
Power Supply PC BoardsProvide or regulate power for selected electronic arrangements.
Control PC BoardsAssist in the management of dedicated elevator control functions.
GEN-II PC BoardsSupport functions associated with selected GEN-II elevator configurations.
FAQ

1. How do different PC boards work together in an elevator?
PC boards exchange signals and information to coordinate the operation of various elevator functions and subsystems.

2. Is every PC board responsible for elevator safety?
PC boards perform different roles depending on their design. Some are associated with operational control, while others support communication, braking, door management or other specialised functions.

3. Can two PC boards look similar but perform different functions?
Yes. PC boards may appear similar in design while being programmed or configured for different functions within the elevator system.

4. Do all elevators use the same types of PC boards?
No. The types of PC boards used may vary depending on the elevator model, technology and control arrangement.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Guide Shoe Insert

Smooth elevator travel depends on the accurate guidance of the elevator car and counterweight along their guide rails. The range of Schindler Elevator Guide Shoe Insert products, guide shoes, rollers and related guide shoe products is used within these guidance arrangements to support alignment and controlled movement during elevator operation.
Schindler Guide Shoe Inserts are available in different sizes and configurations for selected elevator systems.
Available Product Options

Product CategorySpecifications
Guide Shoe Inserts220×10mm
101×10mm
101×16mm

220×30mm 16mm

Guide Shoe Rubber CushionsRear Cushion 40×15mm M10
Side Cushion 40×18mm M10
Note
Additional guide shoe-related variants may be available for selected elevator systems.
Functions

• Associated with the guidance arrangement of elevator cars and counterweights.
  • Support controlled movement along vertical guide rails.
  • Contribute to alignment and guidance functions during elevator operation.
  • Assist in maintaining stable movement within selected elevator systems.
  • Support the operation of guide shoe assemblies and related guide shoe products.
FAQ

1. Why are guide shoes important in an elevator system?
Guide shoes help keep the elevator car and counterweight aligned with their guide rails during movement.

2. Why do elevators use separate guide shoes for the car and counterweight?
The elevator car and counterweight move independently on their respective guide rails and may require dedicated guide shoe arrangements.

3. What is the difference between a guide shoe insert and a complete guide shoe?
A guide shoe insert forms part of the guide shoe arrangement, while a complete guide shoe consists of multiple components working together.

4. Why are rollers used in some guide shoe arrangements?
Guide shoe rollers are used to facilitate smooth movement along elevator guide rails within selected elevator guidance arrangements.
