groups.meta_description: Buy Schindler elevator guide shoe rollers designed to support smooth and stable elevator movement. Find reliable elevator guide shoe rollers in the UAE.
groups.keywords: Schindler Guide Shoe Roller, Schindler Elevator Guide Shoe Roller, Elevator Guide Shoe Rollers UAE
paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Guide Shoe Roller

Guide shoe rollers are designed to facilitate the guided movement of elevator components while maintaining proper contact with the guide rails. Their rolling action helps minimise friction, reduce operational noise and support smoother elevator travel. As part of the guide shoe assembly, these rollers play an important role in maintaining consistent elevator performance.

At Bestech, we offer a range of Schindler elevator guide shoe roller solutions suitable for maintenance and replacement requirements. Businesses looking for dependable elevator guide shoe rollers in UAE can explore our collection of guide shoe roller components used across selected Schindler elevator applications.
Available Roller Sizes

• Roller for Guide Shoe - 150 × 30 mm
  • Roller for Guide Shoe - 100 × 25 mm
Functions

• Help reduce friction between the guide shoe assembly and guide rails.
  • Support smooth and stable elevator movement.
  • Assist in minimising vibration and operational noise.
  • Contribute to passenger ride comfort.
  • Support the efficient operation of the guide system.
FAQ

1. Why are guide shoe rollers important in an elevator?
Guide shoe rollers help the elevator move smoothly along the guide rails by reducing friction and supporting stable operation.

2. When should guide shoe rollers be inspected?
Guide shoe rollers should be checked during routine elevator maintenance to identify signs of wear or damage.

3. How do guide shoe rollers differ from guide shoe inserts?
Guide shoe rollers use rolling contact to support movement, while guide shoe inserts provide guided support within the guide shoe assembly.

4. Do guide shoe rollers affect passenger comfort?
Yes. Properly functioning rollers help minimise vibration and support a smoother travel experience.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Door Vanes & Door Sliders

The opening and closing of elevator doors involves products that help coordinate movement and engagement between the elevator car door and landing door arrangement. Schindler elevator Door Vane and Door Slider products are used within these door operating systems to facilitate door coupling, guidance and movement functions.
The range includes door vanes, sliders and related door products designed for selected elevator systems.
Available Product Options

Product CategorySpecifications
Door VanePB51 Door Vane
V30 Door Vane Pair (45 × 101 × 401 mm)
QKS9 Door Vane Set (Left + Right)
Door Vane LeversSmall Arm Lever for Door Vane
Big Arm Lever for Door Vane
Door SlidersDoor Slider 74 × 58 × 15 mm
300P Door Slider 22 × 18 × 11 mm
Rubber Door Slider 77 × 60 × 13 mm
Door Slider Plastic 73 × 15 × 15 mm
V30 Door Slider VariantsV30 Door Slider with Iron
V30 Slider 50 × 16 × 10 mm
Product Note
Door vane and door slider products are available in multiple configurations to suit different elevator door arrangements.
Functions
Door Vanes

• Facilitate engagement between elevator car and landing door arrangements.
  • Support door operation when the elevator reaches the designated door zone.
  • Contribute to the interaction between car doors and landing doors during operation.
Door Sliders

• Support door guidance and positioning functions.
  • Assist with door movement within selected door arrangements.
  • Contribute to the operation of slider-based door systems.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a door vane in an elevator system?
A door vane is associated with the engagement between the elevator car door and the landing door arrangement when the elevator is positioned in the door zone.

2. What happens when the elevator reaches the door zone?
When the elevator reaches the designated door zone, the door vane engages with the landing door arrangement as part of the door operating process.

3. Why does an elevator door need both a door vane and a door slider?
Door vanes and door sliders perform different functions within the door system. Together, they support door engagement, guidance and movement during elevator operation.

4. Do elevator door systems use the same products on every floor?
The door operating arrangement may vary depending on the elevator design and system configuration used within the building.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Push Buttons

Schindler elevator push buttons are essential interface components that allow passengers to interact with the elevator system. Installed within elevator operating panels, these buttons are used to select floors and activate specific elevator functions.

At Bestech, we offer a range of Schindler elevator push buttons suitable for maintenance and replacement requirements. Businesses looking for dependable elevator push buttons in UAE solutions can explore our collection of Schindler elevator button parts used across selected Schindler elevator applications.
Available Button Types

• D-Type Push Button
  • M-Type Push Button (Screw Mounting)
  • Illuminated Push Button - 50 × 25 × 34 mm
  • M-Type Push Button (Clip Mounting)
Why Are Elevator Push Buttons Important?

• Enable passengers to select floors and elevator functions.
  • Provide a convenient interface for elevator operation.
  • Support clear user interaction with elevator systems.
  • Available in different mounting and design configurations.
  • Contribute to efficient day-to-day elevator use.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of an elevator push button?
Elevator push buttons allow passengers to select floors and interact with elevator functions through the operating panel.

2. What is the difference between screw-mounted and clip-mounted push buttons?
The difference lies in the installation method, with each type designed for specific elevator panel configurations.

3. Why are illuminated push buttons used in elevators?
Illuminated buttons improve visibility and help passengers identify button locations more easily.

4. Can elevator push buttons display floor numbers?
Yes. Depending on the design, elevator buttons may include floor numbers, symbols or other indicators for user guidance.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Hanger Roller & Sheave
Hanger Rollers

The Schindler elevator hanger rollers form part of the elevator door hanger assembly and play an important role in supporting smooth door movement. Proper alignment of the hanger assembly helps ensure that the door vane is correctly positioned to engage with landing door lock rollers (interlocks).
Accurate positioning contributes to reliable door operation and proper interlock engagement.
Sheaves

A sheave is a pulley component used within elevator systems to guide and support the movement of ropes or cables. In Schindler elevator applications, sheaves may be used as traction sheaves or diverter sheaves, depending on the system design. These components are important for the efficient operation of elevator traction and suspension systems.
Businesses looking for Schindler Elevator Sheave in UAE solutions can explore our range of sheaves and related components suitable for selected elevator applications.
Available Product Range
Hanger Rollers

• Hanger Roller - QD 76 mm
  • Hanger Roller - 73 × 13 mm, Length 125 mm
  • Hanger Roller - 73 × 15 mm, Length 125 mm
  • Φ72 Core Centre Hanger Roller
  • T-Shaped Hanger Roller - 54 × 18 mm, GD48, 6200, ID:10
  • ADP Door-Type Hanger Roller - 72 × 15 mm, GD64, 6200
  • SOPI Door-Type Under Hanger Roller - 34 × 19 mm, GD28, 608
Sheaves

• Sheave - 60 × 11 mm, 6200, GD:48, ID:10
  • Sheave Unit - 50 × 10 mm, GD:36
  • Sheave Hanger (Pair) - 92 × 55 × 14 mm
  • Traction Sheave - 720 × 160 × 7 (13,13,13,14,13,13,13)
Functions
Hanger Rollers

• Support smooth movement of elevator doors.
  • Assist in maintaining proper hanger assembly alignment.
  • Help position the door vane for correct interlock engagement.
  • Contribute to reliable door operation.
Sheaves

• Guide and support the movement of elevator ropes or cables.
  • Assist in traction and suspension system operation.
  • Support efficient rope movement within the elevator system.
  • Used in selected traction and diverter sheave applications.
FAQ

1. What is the function of a hanger roller in an elevator?
A hanger roller helps support the smooth movement of elevator doors while assisting with proper door alignment.

2. Why is hanger roller alignment important?
Correct alignment helps ensure that the door vane engages properly with the landing door lock system for reliable door operation.

3. What is a sheave in an elevator system?
A sheave is a pulley component that guides elevator ropes or cables and supports traction system operation.

4. What is the difference between a traction sheave and a diverter sheave?
A traction sheave transfers driving force to move the elevator, while a diverter sheave helps guide and redirect the ropes within the system.

faq.answer: A hanger roller helps support the smooth movement of elevator doors while assisting with proper door alignment.

faq.answer: Correct alignment helps ensure that the door vane engages properly with the landing door lock system for reliable door operation.

faq.answer: A sheave is a pulley component that guides elevator ropes or cables and supports traction system operation.

faq.question: What is the difference between a traction sheave and a diverter sheave?
faq.answer: A traction sheave transfers driving force to move the elevator, while a diverter sheave helps guide and redirect the ropes within the system.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Door Lock & Power Lock Systems
1. Door Locks

Elevator landing doors must remain mechanically locked when the elevator car is not present at the landing. Door lock components help ensure that the locking mechanism is properly engaged, allowing the door to remain securely closed until safe operation conditions are met. Correct alignment and engagement of the locking system contribute to reliable elevator door operation.
Door Lock Assemblies

• OKS9 (PB260) Door Lock Set - 1 Left + 1 Right
  • OKS9 (PB260) Door Lock Plate Set - 1 Left + 1 Right
  • AD9 Door Lock - 360 × 127 × 62 mm (PB258)
  • V30 (PB257) Door Lock
  • Left-Hand Door Lock
Door Lock Contact Components

• Door Lock Contact - 30 × 27 × 73 mm
  • Door Lock Contact for OK59 Door Lock
Rollers & Locking Wheels

• Door Lock Roller - 31 × 21 mm (ID:14) and 40 × 28 mm
  • Door Lock Wheel - 30 × 15 mm (ID:12), 26 × 14 mm (ID:9) and 31 × 21 mm (ID:12)
2. Power Locks

Power locks are key-operated switch components used to control electrical power to the elevator system or selected circuits. These components assist authorised personnel in managing power access during maintenance and service procedures.
Available Power Lock Variants

• 300# Power Lock
  • 400# Power Lock
  • 500# Power Lock
3. Door Buffers
Door lock buffers are used within door lock arrangements to help interrupt electrical circuits under abnormal operating conditions.
Available Door Buffer Product
• Door Lock Buffer (SCBU-1)
4. Circuit Breakers
Circuit breakers are protective electrical components used to help safeguard elevator systems by interrupting electrical circuits under abnormal operating conditions.
Available Product Options
• Circuit Breaker IDNR.181369 - Secondary Forced Circuit Breaker
5. Open Devices
Schindler open devices are used within selected elevator door operating arrangements and are associated with door opening functions.
The range includes Open Device and Open Door Disconnect Device variants for selected elevator door operating arrangements.
Key Functions
Locking & Safety Functions

• Help maintain secure landing door locking arrangements.
  • Support the engagement and operation of door locking systems.
Power Management Functions

• Assist with controlled access to electrical power systems.
  • Support maintenance and service activities.
Door Protection Functions

• Help cushion door movement and reduce impact during operation.
  • Contribute to smoother door system performance.
Electrical Protection Functions

• Support the interruption of electrical circuits under abnormal conditions.
  • Contribute to the protection of elevator electrical systems.
Door Operation Functions

• Assist with selected door opening and operating functions.
  • Support the performance of elevator door systems.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of an elevator door lock?
An elevator door lock helps keep landing doors securely closed when the elevator car is not present, supporting safe elevator operation.

2. What is a power lock used for in an elevator?
A power lock is typically a key-operated switch used to control electrical power to the elevator system or selected circuits.

3. Why is proper door lock alignment important?
Proper alignment helps ensure that the locking mechanism engages correctly and supports reliable door operation.

4. What does a circuit breaker do in an elevator system?
A circuit breaker helps protect elevator electrical circuits by interrupting power during abnormal operating conditions.

5. What is an open device in an elevator?
An open device is used within selected elevator door operating arrangements and is associated with door opening functions.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Contactor & Controller

Schindler elevator control and contactor components are essential parts of elevator electrical systems, helping regulate power distribution, motor control and overall system performance. Commonly used in maintenance and replacement projects, these components support the efficient operation of selected elevator installations.

Companies looking for Schindler elevator contactor parts and Schindler elevator control components can find a selection of compatible replacement solutions designed for various Schindler elevator applications.
Product Overview
Contactors

A contactor is a heavy-duty electromechanical switch used to control high-power electrical circuits within an elevator system. In Schindler elevator applications, contactors are commonly used to manage the main drive motor and brake circuits, supporting controlled elevator operation.
Controllers

In older Schindler elevator systems, silicon controller technology typically refers to a Silicon Controlled Rectifier (SCR) drive system, also known as a thyristor-based drive system. These controller components are used to regulate and manage electrical power within the elevator drive system.
Product Categories

CategoryAvailable Products
ContactorsLCL-D25 Contactor
MY N Contactor LCL-D32 AC Contactor
Controller ComponentsMCG 132-12bd 312C Silicon Controlled Rectifier (SCR)
Functions
Contactors

• Control high-power electrical circuits.
  • Support operation of elevator drive motors.
  • Assist in managing brake circuit functions.
  • Contribute to controlled elevator operation.
Controller Components

• Assist in regulating electrical power within the drive system.
  • Support drive system control functions.
  • Form part of thyristor-based elevator control systems.
  • Contribute to the management of elevator drive operations.
FAQ

1. What is a contactor in an elevator system?
A contactor is a heavy-duty electromechanical switch used to control high-power circuits such as elevator drive motors and brake systems.

2. What does a silicon controller do in an elevator?
A silicon controller helps regulate and manage electrical power within the elevator drive system.

3. What is an SCR drive system?
An SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) drive system is a thyristor-based control system used in certain elevator drive applications.

4. Why are contactors important in elevator operation?
Contactors help control electrical circuits required for elevator movement and braking functions.

faq.question: What is a contactor in an elevator system?
faq.answer: A contactor is a heavy-duty electromechanical switch used to control high-power circuits such as elevator drive motors and brake systems.

faq.question: What does a silicon controller do in an elevator?
faq.answer: A silicon controller helps regulate and manage electrical power within the elevator drive system.

faq.question: What is an SCR drive system?
faq.answer: An SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) drive system is a thyristor-based control system used in certain elevator drive applications.

faq.question: Why are contactors important in elevator operation?
faq.answer: Contactors help control electrical circuits required for elevator movement and braking functions.

paragraph_text: Schindler Elevator Switches & Safety

Schindler elevator switches and safety devices are used in various elevator control and monitoring applications. These components support functions such as door operation, position detection and system safety, helping elevators operate efficiently and reliably across different environments.
We offer a selection of replacement components for businesses seeking Schindler elevator door switches in the UAE.
Product Overview
Elevator Switches

Elevator switches are electrical components used to detect, monitor or control specific elevator functions. Many switches operate using a simple electrical configuration that requires only two conductors to complete or interrupt a circuit. These components are commonly used in door systems, positioning functions and operational controls within elevator systems.
Safety Switches

Safety switches are designed to support the safe operation of elevator systems by monitoring specific conditions and helping ensure that components operate within their intended parameters. They form part of the overall safety and protection mechanisms used in elevator installations.
Available Product Options

CategoryAvailable Variants
Limit SwitchesUKT Limit Switch Automatic Return - 109 × 31 × 30 mm
UKS Limit Switch Manual Return - 109 × 31 × 30 mm
Safety SwitchesSecurity Window Safety Switch
Functions
Elevator Switches

• Monitor and control specific elevator functions.
  • Complete or interrupt electrical circuits as required.
  • Support elevator door and operational control systems.
  • Contribute to reliable elevator performance.
Safety Switches

• Monitor operating conditions within elevator systems.
  • Support safety and protection functions.
  • Assist in maintaining safe elevator operation.
  • Contribute to system reliability.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of an elevator switch?
An elevator switch is used to monitor, detect or control specific functions within the elevator system, such as door operation or positioning functions.

2. What is a limit switch in an elevator?
A limit switch is designed to detect a specific position or operating limit and help control elevator functions accordingly.

3. Are switches used only for door operation?
No. Elevator switches can be used for various functions, including position detection, operational control and safety monitoring.

4. Are limit switches and safety switches the same?
No. Limit switches are generally used to detect operating limits or positions, while safety switches are used to support safety-related functions.

1. Schindler Door Cables

Schindler door cable products are used to mechanically link the movement of elevator car doors and landing doors within selected door operating arrangements. These products contribute to the coordinated movement of door systems and are available in multiple lengths and configurations for selected elevator applications.

3. Schindler Door Hanger Panel

Schindler door hanger panels are structural products used to support elevator door panels and facilitate movement along the door track arrangement. These products form part of selected elevator door systems and contribute to door support and guidance functions.

The door motor provides the movement required for door operation, while the door cable helps coordinate movement within selected door arrangements.

2. What is the purpose of a door hanger panel in an elevator system?
A door hanger panel supports the door panel and allows it to move along the door track arrangement during operation.

3. Why do elevator door systems use brake-related products such as magnetic drums?
Brake-related products are used within selected door motor arrangements to support door operating functions.

4. Can elevator door systems contain both mechanical and electrical products?
Yes. Elevator door arrangements may include mechanical products responsible for movement and support, together with electrical products associated with door operation and control functions.

Schindler elevator inverter and PCB products are used within elevator systems to support motor control, communication, monitoring and operational functions. The range includes Schindler elevator inverter products, PCB boards, communication boards, power supply boards and specialised electronic boards used within selected elevator arrangements.

Inverter Power Supply Boards

FAQ

Different electronic boards are designed to perform specific functions such as motor control, communication, monitoring and operational management.

2. Why are PCB boards identified using reference numbers?
Reference numbers help identify the correct board for product selection, replacement and compatibility purposes.

3. Can an elevator system contain multiple PCB boards?
Yes. Elevator systems commonly use multiple electronic boards that perform different functions within the overall control arrangement.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Push Buttons

Every elevator journey begins with a simple press of a button. Push buttons serve as the primary communication point between passengers and the elevator system, making them one of the most frequently used components in any building.

In Thyssenkrupp elevator systems, push buttons are designed to combine functionality, durability and modern aesthetics while providing a comfortable user experience. From landing stations to car operating panels, these buttons help passengers register calls, select floors and interact with the elevator system efficiently. Modern designs also support accessibility requirements through clear markings, tactile features and user-friendly operation.
Available Product Options

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Push ButtonsPush Button 32.6 × 32.6 (THBT01), Push Button 36.8 × 36.8 (THBT02)
Additional Variants

The Thyssenkrupp Elevator Push Button range includes compact button designs suitable for different operating panels and landing stations. Available variants are manufactured to accommodate varying panel layouts while maintaining consistent functionality, visual appeal and ease of use across elevator installations.
Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Push Buttons

• Register passenger floor selections.
  • Enable elevator call requests.
  • Support car and landing operation panels.
  • Improve passenger interaction with the system.
  • Assist accessibility and usability requirements.
  • Provide clear and responsive operation.
  • Withstand frequent daily use.
  • Contribute to a modern elevator appearance.
FAQ

1. What are Thyssenkrupp Elevator Push Buttons?
Push buttons are user-operated controls that allow passengers to call an elevator and select their desired floor.

2. Where are elevator push buttons installed?
They are commonly installed on landing panels outside the elevator and on operating panels inside the elevator car.

3. Are elevator push buttons designed for frequent use?
Yes. Elevator push buttons are manufactured to handle continuous operation in residential, commercial and public buildings.

4. Do modern elevator push buttons support accessibility features?
Many modern push button designs include tactile markings, clear identification and user-friendly features to improve accessibility for passengers.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Rollers & Guide Shoe Rollers
Door rollers and guide shoe rollers are designed to reduce friction, maintain alignment, and support controlled movement throughout the elevator system.

In Thyssenkrupp elevator systems, door rollers help car and landing doors move effortlessly along their tracks, reducing vibration and noise during opening and closing cycles. Guide shoe rollers perform an important task by guiding the elevator car and counterweight along the vertical guide rails.
Together, these components contribute to stable travel, accurate positioning and consistent performance in residential, commercial and high-rise applications.
Available Product Options

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Door RollersDoor Roller Φ30 × 9 × M10 (THWR01), Door Roller Φ70 × 17 × 6200 R-Groove (THWR02), Door Roller Φ70 × 17 × 6200 S-Groove (THWR03), Door Roller Φ80 × 13.5 × 6200 (THWR04)
Guide Shoe RollersGuide Shoe Roller Φ153 × 32 × 2-6204 (THWR05), Guide Shoe Roller Φ82 × 24 × 2-6201 (THWR06), Guide Shoe Roller Φ100 × 34 × M16 (THWR07)
Roller Guide Shoe AssembliesRoller Guide Shoe Assembly, Roller Φ153 × 32 × 2-6204, Mounting Hole Φ140 × 75 (THWR08)
Additional Variants

The available range includes door rollers for various door operator systems. Variations in roller diameter, groove profile, bearing configuration and mounting dimensions allow these components to be matched to specific Thyssenkrupp elevator models and operating conditions.
Key Functions

• Reduce friction and mechanical wear.
  • Help maintain proper alignment of moving components.
  • Improve ride comfort by minimizing vibration.
  • Enable stable guidance along elevator rails.
  • Contribute to quieter elevator operation.
  • Enhance overall system reliability.
  • Support long-term performance of door and guidance systems.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a door roller in an elevator?
Door rollers help elevator doors move smoothly along their tracks, supporting quiet and reliable opening and closing operation.

2. What does a guide shoe roller do?
A guide shoe roller helps guide the elevator car or counterweight along the guide rails while maintaining stability during travel.

3. Why are different roller sizes available?
Different elevator systems require specific roller dimensions and configurations to match door mechanisms and guide rail designs.

4. What is a roller guide shoe assembly?
A roller guide shoe assembly combines rollers and mounting components into a complete unit used to guide the elevator along the rails.

5. Are door rollers and guide shoe rollers used for the same purpose?
No. Door rollers assist door movement, while guide shoe rollers are responsible for guiding the elevator car and counterweight along the shaft rails.

groups.meta_title: Guide Shoe Components for Thyssenkrupp Elevators | Bestech
groups.meta_description: Get guide shoe inserts and assemblies that help reduce vibration, maintain rail contact and support smoother elevator travel.
groups.keywords: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Guide Shoe, Thyssenkrupp Guide Shoe Inserts, Elevator Guide Shoe Parts UAE
paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Guide Shoes & Guide Shoe Inserts

An elevator may travel hundreds of times each day and maintaining a smooth, stable ride depends on precise guidance along the rails. Guide shoes and guide shoe inserts are the components responsible for keeping the elevator car and counterweight properly aligned as they move through the shaft.

In Thyssenkrupp elevator systems, guide shoe inserts act as the interface between the guide rail and the moving assembly. They absorb minor vibrations, reduce direct metal-to-metal contact, and help ensure controlled movement throughout the travel path. These components also help protect guide rails from excessive wear, contributing to longer service life and improved ride comfort.
From low-rise buildings to high-traffic commercial installations, properly selected guide shoe components play an important role in maintaining consistent elevator performance and passenger comfort.
Available Product Options

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Guide Shoe InsertsGuide Shoe Insert 150 × 17mm (THGS01), Guide Shoe Insert 125 × 5mm (THGS02), Guide Shoe Insert 48 × 28 × 17mm (THGS03), Guide Shoe Insert 125 × 10mm (THGS04), Guide Shoe Insert 125 × 16mm (THGS05), Guide Shoe Insert 130 × 10mm (THGS06), Guide Shoe Rubber Cushion 118 × 92 × 47mm (THGS07), Guide Shoe Insert 140 × 16/10mm (THGS08), Guide Shoe Insert 220 × 27 × 24 × 10/16mm (THGS09)
Guide Shoe AssembliesGuide Shoe Assembly 125 × 10/16, Mounting Hole 100 × 65 (THGS10 - HF-04), Guide Shoe Assembly 200 × 17, Mounting Hole 100 × 65 (THGS11 - HF-05), Guide Shoe Assembly 130 × 10, Mounting Hole 118 × 60 (THGS12 - HF-03), Guide Shoe Assembly 130 × 10, Mounting Hole 60 (THGS13 - HF-02), Guide Shoe Assembly 140 × 16, Mounting Hole 89 × 85 (THGS14 - HF-01)
Additional Variants

The available range covers both replacement inserts and complete guide shoe assemblies suited to different rail profiles and installation requirements. While insert dimensions vary based on guide rail specifications, assembly variants are designed with different mounting configurations to match elevator car and counterweight applications. Rubber cushioning options are also available to help absorb movement-related vibration and enhance ride smoothness.
Functions
Guide shoe inserts are constantly in contact with the guide rails, making them one of the key components influencing ride quality. Properly maintained inserts help:

• Keep the elevator car aligned during travel.
  • Reduce vibration and rattling within the shaft.
  • Minimize wear on guide rails.
  • Support quieter operation.
  • Improve passenger comfort.
  • Maintain stable movement at varying speeds.
  • Protect surrounding mechanical components.
  • Extend the lifespan of the guiding system.
FAQ

1. What is the purpose of a guide shoe insert?
A guide shoe insert helps the elevator car travel smoothly along the guide rails while reducing friction and vibration during operation.

2. How does a worn guide shoe insert affect elevator performance?
Excessive wear can lead to increased vibration, noise, uneven movement and reduced ride comfort.

3. What is a guide shoe assembly?
A guide shoe assembly is the complete guiding unit that holds the insert and helps maintain proper alignment between the elevator and guide rails.

4. Do guide shoe inserts help reduce noise?
Yes. By minimizing vibration and maintaining consistent rail contact, guide shoe inserts contribute to quieter elevator operation.

groups.meta_title: Door Slider & Door Vane Solutions for Thyssenkrupp Elevators
groups.meta_description: Door sliders and vanes engineered to support smooth door guidance, proper alignment and dependable operation in Thyssenkrupp elevator systems.
groups.keywords: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Slider, Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Vane, Elevator Door Slider and Door Vane UAE
paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Slider & Door Vane
Smaller mechanical components such as door sliders and door vanes play an important role in guiding door movement and supporting reliable door operation.
A Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Slider, often referred to as a door guide shoe, helps guide the door panel along its track while reducing friction during opening and closing.
A Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Vane is mounted on the elevator car and works as part of the door operating mechanism, helping coordinate the interaction between the car doors and landing doors.
Together, these components support stable door movement, reduce unnecessary wear and help ensure smooth passenger access across various elevator applications.
Available Product Options

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Door SlidersDoor Slider 48 × 20 × 12 × Ø5 (THDS01), Door Slider 48 × 20 × 12 (THDS02), Door Slider 41 × 19 × 11.5 × Ø8 (THDS03), Door Slider 41 × 19 × 11.5 × Ø8 (THDS04), Door Slider 35 × 92 × 13 (THDS05), Door Slider 90 × 13 × 3.5 (THDS06), Door Slider 36 × 93 × 13 (THDS07), Door Slider 102 × 15 × 13 (THDS08), Door Slider 70 × 40 × 14 (THDS09), Door Slider 44 × 27 × 13 (THDS10), Door Slider 70 × 42 × 12.5 (THDS11)
Door VanesDoor Vane S8 (THDV01), Door Vane F3 (THDV02)
Additional Variants

The available door slider range includes multiple sizes and profiles designed to suit different Thyssenkrupp door operator assemblies. Depending on the elevator model, sliders may vary in dimensions, mounting style and guide channel configuration to ensure proper door alignment and smooth movement.
The range also includes S8 and F3 Door Vanes, which are commonly used within door coupling mechanisms to support accurate engagement between the elevator car doors and landing doors during operation.
Functions of Door Sliders and Door Vanes
Thyssenkrupp Door Sliders and Door Vanes help with:

• Guiding door panels during operation.
  • Supporting smooth opening and closing movement.
  • Maintaining proper door alignment.
  • Reducing friction and component wear.
  • Assisting interaction between car and landing doors.
  • Supporting reliable door operator performance.
  • Enhancing passenger convenience.
  • Contributing to long-term door system durability.
FAQ

1. What is a door slider in an elevator?
A door slider is a low-friction component that helps guide elevator door panels along their intended path during opening and closing.

2. What is the purpose of a door vane?
A door vane helps coordinate the operation of elevator car doors and landing doors, ensuring proper door engagement during operation.

3. Why are door sliders important?
Door sliders help maintain smooth door movement, proper alignment and reduced wear on door components.

4. Can worn door sliders affect elevator performance?
Yes. Damaged or worn sliders can lead to misalignment, increased friction and less efficient door operation.

5. Are door sliders and door vanes used in all elevator systems?
The design may vary by model, but both components are commonly used in elevator door systems to support safe and reliable door operation.

groups.meta_title: Door Safety & Access Components for Thyssenkrupp Elevators
groups.meta_description: Browse door locks, power locks and safety locking components for TK Elevator systems. Designed to support secure and reliable door operation.
groups.keywords: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Lock, Thyssenkrupp Power Lock, Elevator Door Lock Parts UAE
paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Lock & Power Lock

Elevator doors are the primary safety barrier between passengers and the hoist-way. The Thyssenkrupp elevator door lock is one of the most critical safety devices within the elevator system. It secures the landing doors and confirms that they are properly locked before elevator movement is permitted. This safety function helps protect passengers and supports compliance with elevator safety requirements.

In addition to standard door locks, specialized components such as triangle locks provide authorized emergency access to landing doors when required by trained personnel. Power lock systems further enhance operational safety by actively controlling or monitoring locking mechanisms through electrical and electromechanical functions.
Available Product Options

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Door LocksLanding Door Lock K8, Car Door Lock K8
Emergency Access LocksTriangle Lock
Power Lock SystemsPower Lock
Locking System Variants

The Thyssenkrupp door locking range includes several safety-focused components designed for elevator door security and operational control. Common variants include Landing Door Lock K8, Car Door Lock K8, Triangle Lock and Power Lock.
These components are used across various TK Elevator systems to support landing door interlocking, controlled access, door status verification and safe elevator operation.
Functions of Door Locks and Power Locks
Thyssenkrupp Door Lock and Power Lock components help with:

• Securing landing and car doors during operation.
  • Preventing unauthorized door opening.
  • Supporting elevator safety interlock functions.
  • Verifying door lock status before movement.
  • Providing controlled emergency door access.
  • Assisting safe passenger entry and exit.
  • Supporting compliance with elevator safety standards.
  • Enhancing overall system reliability.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Lock?
A door lock, also known as a landing door interlock, is a safety device that prevents elevator doors from opening when the elevator is not correctly positioned at a landing.

2. Why are door locks important in elevators?
Door locks play a key role in passenger safety by ensuring doors remain secure during travel and open only when the elevator reaches the correct floor.

3. What is a triangle lock used for?
A triangle lock provides authorized emergency access to landing doors and is typically operated using a specialized triangular key.

4. What does a power lock do in an elevator system?
A power lock helps control or monitor door locking functions through electrical or electromechanical mechanisms, supporting safe operation.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Sensors and Switches
Sensors and switches continuously monitor the movement and location of elevator components, helping the control system make precise decisions throughout every journey.
A levelling sensor helps ensure that the elevator car stops precisely at the landing floor, allowing passengers to enter and exit safely.
Magnetic switches provide non-contact sensing by detecting magnetic fields, making them suitable for position monitoring and control applications.
Inspection switches are commonly used during maintenance activities, giving technicians a controlled way to perform servicing and inspection procedures.
Reliable Thyssenkrupp elevator sensors and switches contribute to smoother operation, improved safety and consistent system performance across a wide range of elevator installations.
Available Product Options

Sensor & Switch TypeAvailable Variants
Levelling SensorsLevelling Sensor CEDES/GLS/TK, Levelling Sensor LK-LN, Levelling Sensor Autonics
Magnetic SwitchesMagnet Switch BN325-R

Sensor and Switch Variants
The Thyssenkrupp Elevator Sensor Parts range includes specialized sensing and switching components used for elevator positioning, levelling, monitoring and maintenance operations.

Common variants include Levelling Sensor CEDES/GLS/TK, Levelling Sensor LK-LN, Levelling Sensor Autonics, Magnet Switch BN325-R and Inspection Switch LW39-16B-6AC-55X/5P. These components help support accurate floor alignment, position detection, operational monitoring and safe servicing procedures within TK Elevator systems.
Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Sensors and Switches
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Sensors and Switches help with:

• Ensuring accurate floor levelling.
  • Detecting elevator car position.
  • Monitoring movement and travel direction.
  • Supporting door and control system operations.
  • Providing non-contact magnetic sensing.
  • Assisting in maintenance and inspection activities.
  • Enhancing passenger safety and comfort.
  • Contributing to reliable elevator performance.
FAQ

1. What is a levelling sensor in an elevator?
A levelling sensor is a device that helps ensure the elevator car stops accurately at the landing floor for safe passenger access.

2. How does a magnetic switch work in an elevator system?
A magnetic switch detects the presence of a magnetic field and sends a signal to the control system for position monitoring or operational control.

3. Why are sensors important in elevator systems?
Sensors provide critical feedback about position, movement and system status, helping maintain safe and efficient operation.

4. What is an inspection switch used for?
An inspection switch allows technicians to safely perform maintenance, testing and servicing procedures on the elevator system.

5. Can faulty sensors affect elevator performance?
Yes. A malfunctioning sensor can impact levelling accuracy, position detection and overall system reliability, making timely replacement important.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Oil Box & Inspection Box

In Thyssenkrupp Elevator systems, oil boxes, inspection boxes and brake control units perform important functions that help maintain equipment condition, assist technicians during servicing and support critical safety operations.

The Thyssenkrupp elevator oil box, often referred to as a guide rail lubricator or oil pot, helps ensure consistent lubrication of the guide rails. Proper lubrication reduces friction between moving components and contributes to smoother elevator travel while minimizing wear over time.

The Thyssenkrupp inspection box is an essential safety station typically installed within the elevator pit. It provides maintenance personnel with access to inspection controls, allowing technicians to perform servicing and troubleshooting procedures safely during maintenance operations.

Product TypeAvailable Variants
Oil Box AssembliesOil Box, Oil Box With Guide Shoe
Pit Inspection BoxesPit Inspection Box, Pit Inspection Box RFT12-F

Functions of Oil Boxes, Inspection Boxes and Brake Controllers
These components help with:

• Providing lubrication to elevator guide rails.
  • Reducing friction and wear on moving components.
  • Supporting smooth elevator travel.
  • Enabling safe maintenance and inspection procedures.
  • Providing technician access to inspection controls.
  • Assisting troubleshooting and servicing activities.
  • Managing electromagnetic brake operation.
  • Supporting elevator safety and operational reliability.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp Elevator Oil Box?
An oil box, also known as a guide rail lubricator or oil pot, is a reservoir that supplies lubricant to elevator guide rails to reduce friction and wear.

2. What is the purpose of an inspection box in an elevator pit?
The inspection box provides maintenance personnel with access to inspection controls, allowing safe servicing and troubleshooting of the elevator system.

3. What does a brake controller do?
A brake controller manages the operation of the traction machine's electromagnetic brake, helping ensure controlled stopping and safe elevator operation.

4. Where is the pit inspection box located?
It is typically installed inside the elevator pit, often mounted on a side wall near the access ladder for convenient maintenance access.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Test Tools

Keeping an elevator operating at its best requires more than routine maintenance. Technicians often need specialized tools to access system settings, monitor performance and troubleshoot operational issues. This is where Thyssenkrupp elevator test tools play an important role.

In Thyssenkrupp elevator systems, parameter testing is commonly used to review, configure and fine-tune operational settings related to motor performance, travel characteristics, safety functions and controller behavior.

groups.meta_title: Encoder & Contactor Components for Thyssenkrupp Elevators | Bestech
groups.meta_description: Discover encoder and contactor components for Thyssenkrupp Elevator systems. It supports precise motion monitoring, motor control and safe power management.
groups.keywords: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Encoder and Contactor, Thyssenkrupp Encoder Parts, Elevator Encoder and Contactor UAE
paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Encoder and Contactor

A Thyssenkrupp Elevator Encoder and Contactor combination helps the control system monitor movement while managing the flow of electrical power to key components. The encoder acts as a precision sensing device, converting mechanical movement into digital signals that allow the controller to track speed, position and direction.

Simultaneously, contactors function as heavy-duty electromagnetic switches that safely connect or disconnect power to motors and other electrical equipment when required. These components are widely used across various Thyssenkrupp elevator models designed for residential, commercial, industrial and high-rise applications.
Available Product Options

Component TypeAvailable Variants
Encoder UnitsEncoder ECN 1313, Encoder HG 900E, Encoder ECN-113, Encoder 4096, Encoder
Encoder ModelsModel BSM150GB120DN2, Model SR2
ContactorContactor SR1
Encoder and Contactor Variants

The Thyssenkrupp elevator encoder and contactor range includes a variety of components designed to support motion feedback, position monitoring and electrical switching functions within TK Elevator systems.
Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Encoders and Contactors
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Encoder and Contactor components help with:

• Monitoring elevator speed and travel position.
  • Providing accurate motion feedback to the controller.
  • Tracking motor rotation and direction of movement.
  • Controlling the flow of electrical power to major components.
  • Supporting safe motor operation and switching functions.
  • Improving operational accuracy and system reliability.
  • Contributing to smooth and efficient elevator performance.
FAQ

1. What is an encoder in a Thyssenkrupp Elevator system?
An encoder is an electronic sensor that converts mechanical movement into digital signals, allowing the control system to monitor speed, position and direction.

2. Why is an encoder important in an elevator?
The encoder provides the feedback needed for accurate positioning, smooth travel and precise floor leveling.

3. What does a contactor do in an elevator?
A contactor is an electromagnetic switching device that controls the flow of electrical power to motors and other major elevator components.

4. How do encoders and contactors work together?
The encoder provides movement data to the controller, while the contactor helps manage the electrical power required for elevator operation.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Motor

The Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Motor is a key component responsible for opening and closing elevator doors safely and efficiently. It is designed to deliver controlled movement throughout thousands of operating cycles.

Whether installed in residential buildings, offices, hotels, hospitals or commercial facilities, these motors help maintain smooth door operation while supporting passenger safety and efficient traffic flow. High-quality Thyssenkrupp Door Motor Parts help maintain operational efficiency while minimizing wear on the overall door system.

Door Motor Variants

Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Motors
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Motors help with:

• Powering elevator door opening and closing operations.
  • Supporting automatic door operator systems.
  • Ensuring smooth and controlled door movement.
  • Assisting with accurate door positioning.
  • Enhancing passenger safety and convenience.
  • Supporting high-frequency operating cycles.
  • Reducing wear on door system components.
  • Contributing to efficient elevator operation.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp Elevator Door Motor?
A door motor is the primary drive component that powers the opening and closing mechanism of an elevator door system.

2. Where is the door motor installed?
The motor is installed within the door operator assembly, which controls the movement of elevator doors.

3. Why is the door motor important?
It ensures smooth, reliable and safe door operation, which is essential for passenger safety and elevator efficiency.

4. What components work with the door motor?
The door motor typically works alongside control boards, belts, gearboxes, sensors and other door operator components.

5. Can elevator door motors be replaced?
Yes. Compatible replacement motors can be selected based on the elevator model, door operator system and technical requirements.

groups.keywords: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Frequency Inverter, Thyssenkrupp Elevator Inverter Drive, Elevator Frequency Inverter UAE
paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Frequency Inverter

The Thyssenkrupp elevator frequency inverter is a critical component responsible for controlling the speed, acceleration and deceleration of the elevator's traction motor. It plays a key role in delivering smooth starts, precise floor leveling and comfortable ride quality. As the primary motor control device within TK Elevator systems, the inverter helps optimize performance while improving operational efficiency.

A Thyssenkrupp elevator inverter drive continuously adjusts motor voltage and frequency based on elevator demand, ensuring controlled movement and reduced mechanical stress on system components. These drives are widely used in residential, commercial and high-rise applications where reliability, energy efficiency and passenger comfort are essential.

Frequency Inverter TypeAvailable Variants

DIC Series InvertersFrequency Inverter DIC-S120P4
K Series InvertersFrequency Inverter K300
Frequency Inverter Variants

These inverter drives are used across various TK Elevator systems to regulate motor speed, improve ride comfort, enhance energy efficiency and support reliable elevator performance.
Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Frequency Inverters
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Frequency Inverters help with:

• Controlling traction motor speed and torque.
  • Providing smooth acceleration and deceleration.
  • Improving floor leveling accuracy.
  • Enhancing passenger ride comfort.
  • Reducing mechanical wear on elevator components.
  • Supporting energy-efficient elevator operation.
  • Optimizing motor performance under varying loads.
  • Contributing to reliable and consistent elevator movement.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp Elevator Frequency Inverter?
A frequency inverter is a motor control device that regulates voltage and frequency to control the speed and movement of an elevator traction motor.

2. Why is the inverter important in an elevator?
The inverter controls acceleration, deceleration, speed regulation and floor leveling, making it one of the most important components in the elevator system.

3. How does an inverter improve passenger comfort?
By ensuring smooth starts, stops and speed transitions, the inverter helps deliver a comfortable and stable ride experience.

4. Can frequency inverters help reduce energy consumption?
Yes. Modern inverter drives optimize motor operation based on demand, helping improve overall energy efficiency.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Display Board

The Thyssenkrupp elevator display board is an important signalization component used in TK Elevator systems. These display boards provide passengers with real-time information such as floor position, travel direction, elevator status and operational indicators. Designed to enhance the passenger experience, display boards help improve communication between the elevator system and its users.

Whether installed inside the cabin or at landing stations, Thyssenkrupp display board parts support accurate visual signaling and contribute to smooth elevator operation. At Bestech, we offer a range of Elevator Display Boards suitable for different TK Elevator applications.

Display Board TypeAvailable Variants

These Thyssenkrupp display board parts are used in different TK Elevator systems to display floor position, travel direction, operational status and other important passenger information, helping ensure clear communication and a smooth elevator experience.
Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Display Boards
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Display Boards help with:

• Displaying the current floor position to passengers.
  • Indicating the direction of elevator travel (up or down).
  • Providing real-time elevator status information.
  • Supporting passenger communication and way-finding.
  • Improving user experience through clear visual signaling.
  • Assisting smooth passenger movement within buildings.
  • Enhancing visibility in residential, commercial and industrial elevator systems.
  • Supporting efficient elevator monitoring and operation.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp Elevator Display Board?
A display board is a signalization component that shows floor position, direction of travel and elevator status to passengers.

2. Where are elevator display boards installed?
They are commonly installed inside elevator cabins and at landing entrances.

3. What information does a display board show?
Display boards can indicate floor numbers, travel direction, elevator status and operational messages.

4. Are different display board types available?
Yes. LCD displays, LED displays, dot matrix displays and directional indicator boards are commonly used.

5. Can display boards be replaced in existing elevator systems?
Yes. Compatible replacement display boards can be selected based on the elevator model and system requirements.

paragraph_text: Thyssenkrupp Elevator PCB Boards

The Printed Circuit Board (PCB) is one of the most important components in a Thyssenkrupp elevator system. It serves as the electronic backbone of the elevator, helping different components communicate and function together efficiently.

Modern Thyssenkrupp elevators are built with advanced control technologies that rely on multiple specialized boards. Each board is designed to perform a specific task, allowing the elevator to respond accurately to passenger requests while maintaining operational reliability.

The variants are used across various TK Elevator systems to support control processing, communication, signal management, drive operations, safety functions and overall system performance.
Key Functions of Thyssenkrupp Elevator Control Boards
Thyssenkrupp Elevator Control Boards are designed to support a wide range of operational requirements, including:

• Managing elevator control processes.
  • Facilitating communication between system components.
  • Supporting signal processing functions.
  • Assisting with safety monitoring and protection.
  • Supporting drive and motion control systems.
  • Enhancing overall system performance and reliability.
FAQ

1. What is a Thyssenkrupp elevator PCB board?
A Thyssenkrupp elevator PCB board is an electronic circuit board used within TK Elevator systems to control, monitor and manage different elevator functions.

2. What does PCB stand for?
PCB stands for Printed Circuit Board. It is an electronic assembly that connects and supports various components within an elevator control system.

3. Are different PCB boards used for different functions?
Yes. Some boards handle control processing, while others are responsible for communication, safety monitoring, motion control or system interfacing.

4. Why is a compatible PCB board important?
Using the correct PCB board helps ensure proper communication between components, reliable system performance and compatibility with the existing elevator control system.
